authors: N/A

title: A Novel Design of Pulsed Magnet for Repetitive Magnetic Field
authors: Yihang Huang, Huidong Shang, Shaobo Liu, Aoming Ge, Shuang Wang, Liang Li, Yiliang Lv, Tao Peng
abstract: Repetitive pulsed magnetic fields play a critical role in neutron diffraction, room-temperature magnetic refrigeration, and pulsed terahertz gyrotron. In this article, a novel design combining flux concentrator (FC) and coil with cooling channels is proposed. This technique mitigates the thermal load generated in the coil by transferring part of it to the FC, thus enhancing both the magnetic field strength and the repetition rate. Comprehensive simulations of the electromagnetic, thermal, and mechanical behaviors were conducted, showing that the maximum temperature is 430 K and the maximum stress is 57.7MPa. A 10-T pulsed magnetic field at a repetition rate of 5 Hz was successfully generated.

title: Supraharmonic Measurement Based on Windowed Compressive Sensing and Orthogonal Matching Pursuit
authors: Chengbin Liang, Yuanda Liu, Guoping Zhang, Tenghua Yin, Zhaosheng Teng, Jianmin Li, Shiyan Hu
abstract: With the large-scale integration of renewable energy sources and widespread application of high-frequency power electronic devices, the issue of supraharmonics in power systems has become increasingly prominent, which imposes a significant challenge to power quality. To mitigate the impact of spectral leakage and the picket-fence effect on the accuracy of supraharmonic disturbance parameter measurements, and reduce the sampling pressure of supraharmonic signal, this article proposes a supraharmonic measurement method based on windowed compressed sensing (CS) and the orthogonal matching pursuit (OMP) algorithm. The four-term third-order Nuttall window function is integrated into CS technology, where a windowed sparse measurement matrix is constructed to enable windowed compressed sampling of supraharmonic signals. Subsequently, the OMP algorithm is used for frequency estimation of the supraharmonic signals, and the three-spectral-line interpolation technique is applied to reduce the picket-fence effect, improving the accuracy of supraharmonic disturbance frequency, amplitude, and phase measurements. Simulation experiments demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly addresses the spectral leakage and the picket-fence effect, effectively reduces the data required for supraharmonic detection, and improves the measurement accuracy while improving noise resistance. In practical experiments, the absolute errors in frequency and amplitude measurement for three sets of supraharmonic components are 0.55, 0.36, and 1.09 Hz and 0.0006, 0.0008, and 0.001 V, respectively, validating the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed method.

title: Enhancing Wireless Guitar Audio Signal Transmission Using a Flexible Metamaterial-Based Frequency Selective Surface
authors: Omar Shendy, Abeer Elkhouly, Ayesha Wasim Qureshi, Mohd Fareq Abd Malek, Hasliza A Rahim
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: This paper presents the design and practical application of a frequency selective surface (FSS) fabricated using metamaterial fabric (ShieldIt Super) and felt as a flexible substrate. The FSS was designed and simulated in CST Studio Suite to function as a bandpass filter at 2.4 GHz, aligning with the frequency of a wireless audio transmission system. The fabricated FSS was tested in a realistic environment using an electric guitar, a wireless transmitter, and an Arturia MiniFuse 2 audio interface. Audio signals were recorded and analyzed using Audacity. When placed over the receiver, the FSS yielded a significant increase in received signal strength, demonstrating a gain of over 4 dB at both 0.4 m and 4.5 m distances, which were selected to simulate real-world usage conditions. These findings indicate that the flexible FSS structure effectively enhances transmission quality and directionality in non-controlled environments. Further research is recommended to assess its robustness under varying mechanical deformations, such as bending or folding.

title: Self-Detection of Diabetic Foot Ulcer Using Thermography and Deep Learning
authors: Hajer Sulaiman Nasser Al Wahaibi, Taqwa Ali Mohammed Alismaili, Mohammed Abdulla Salim Al Husaini, Yousuf Nasser Al Husaini, Mohamed Hadi Habaebi, Rawad Abdulghafor
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: Diabetic foot ulcers (DFUs) are a severe complication of diabetes, necessitating early detection to mitigate morbidity and mortality. Current diagnostic methods are often manual, time-consuming, and lack real-time automated solutions. This study proposes a deep learning framework leveraging thermal imaging for real-time DFU detection. Utilizing a dataset of 1,800 high-resolution thermal images (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,440 \times 1,080$</tex> pixels) categorized into “Healthy” and “Ulcer” classes, the framework integrates preprocessing techniques—noise reduction, contrast adjustment, and resizing—to optimize input quality. The model's performance is benchmarked against Inception V3 and MobileNet V2, evaluating accuracy, precision, recall, and computational efficiency. Results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves 100% validation accuracy and near-zero loss (0.0006%) by epoch 10, significantly outperforming Inception V3 (86.3% accuracy) and MobileNet V2 (85.55% accuracy). The confusion matrices reveal perfect classification for the proposed model, with no misclassifications, underscoring its clinical reliability.

title: A Cryptographically-Linked Metadata Schema for Academic Credential Interoperability: The ILMEQ Model
authors: Abdulkadir Tasdelen, Mohamed Hadi Habaebi, Md Rafiqul Islam
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: This paper presents ILMEQ (Interlinked Ledger Metadata for Educational Qualifications), a novel metadata schema that enables the representation of academic credentials as cryptographically verifiable digital assets. Aimed at addressing the shortcomings of current credentialing systems—such as lack of interoperability, vulnerability to fraud, and inadequate modeling of document relationships—ILMEQ introduces a JSON-LD-based structure that supports rich semantic linking and integrity validation. Unlike many existing blockchain-based approaches that rely on fully on-chain storage, ILMEQ maintains privacy and scalability by operating off-chain, anchoring only cryptographic hashes of credential metadata. The schema is designed to capture the full lifecycle and interdependencies of academic documents, including prerequisite chains, equivalency certifications, validation references, and revocation effects. It enables status-aware, hashverified validation of credentials without exposing sensitive content, ensuring both trust and confidentiality. ILMEQ was evaluated in three practical educational scenarios, ranging from simple undergraduate records to complex cross-border recognition cases. Results demonstrated successful modeling of linear and non-linear academic paths, effective revocation tracking, and logical propagation of status changes across related documents. The implementation includes a client-side tool for managing and exporting credential graphs, emphasizing modularity, offline capability, and user privacy. By providing a structured, machine-readable, and extensible model for academic credentials, ILMEQ significantly enhances the trustworthiness, portability, and semantic clarity of digital educational records, contributing toward a globally interoperable credentialing infrastructure.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of Energy-Efficient Atmospheric Water Generators for Standalone Applications
authors: Ahmed Badawi, M I. Elzein, Claude Ziad El-Bayeh, Azad Ashraf, Osama Khalid, Nasser Koleed, Mohammed Al-Mass
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: Water scarcity is a critical issue, especially in remote hot areas where many regions are affected by this problem. Over a period of time, the atmosphere is filled with a staggering 3400 trillion gallons of water vapor, which is equivalent to covering the entire planet with a layer of water that is 1 inch thick. In this paper, we introduce a pioneering solution to address water scarceness in areas with moderate to high humidity - Atmospheric Water Generators (AWG). This approach transforms water vapor into liquid water and are specifically created for agrarian and irrigation needs in areas with limited water resources. Two innovative concepts in research paper are introduced, one utilizing Peltier devices and the other a heat exchanger, to investigate various design possibilities. The Peltier-based concept functions by passing current through it, resulting in a temperature difference that cools and condenses the air in its vicinity. The heat exchanger operates by circulating a coolant that is cooled by the lower ground temperature. Both sustainable engineering concepts were created with the goal of minimizing energy usage and expenses, particularly when compared to the existing AWGs available in the market. This research demonstrates that our Peltier-based prototype achieves a rate of 2.5 liters per kWh at tested parameters of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60 \% \text{RH}$</tex> and 85° F, while energy consumption with the heat exchanger is reduced to 0.4 kWh per cubic meter of water. The annual simulations for the climate of Qatar show locally averaged harvesting rates of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.36 ~\mathrm{L} / \text{hr}$</tex> with a production cost of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{ 0. 1 8}$</tex> per liter. Our designs offer a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 \%}$</tex> reduction in upfront costs compared to commercial systems like Gr8water (3,500 gallons/day at around $16,000) and focus on scalable off-grid agricultural use.

title: 5G-NR Resource Management in Multi-Orbit Non-Terrestrial Systems with Multi-Connectivity
authors: Mikko Majamaa, Eva Lagunas, Abuzar B. M. Adam, Flor Ortiz, Lauri Sormunen, Timo Hämäläinen, Symeon Chatzinotas, Tommi Mikkonen
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: Non-terrestrial networks (NTN) have been identified as a key component of the upcoming 5G-Advanced technology, bringing connectivity anywhere on Earth and supporting terrestrial infrastructure whenever needed. Non-geostationary orbit systems such as low-Earth orbit constellations are in the spotlight of this NTN emerging popularity, gathering significant investments in large-scale endeavors. As we move towards systems composed with multiple and dynamical orbital shells, the radio resource block assignment problem becomes challenging. In this work, we formulate the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> compatible resource management problem as a max-min fairness problem applied to the user demand satisfaction metric, also taking into account multi-connectivity where a user can be connected to more than one satellite at a time. Two solution alternatives are provided, namely an optimization-based procedure and a heuristic method. Numerical simulations show that the optimization-based algorithm can fully satisfy 74 % of the users while the heuristic algorithm can satisfy 56% of the users in the considered scenario. The key novelty of this work lies in addressing fairness simultaneously for multiple applications and multiple constellations, distinguishing it from prior approaches that primarily focus on single-application or single-constellation scenarios.

title: On Addressing Bias and Fairness in Large Language Models for Responsible Innovation in Gender Contexts
authors: Varsha Shukla, Geoffrey G Parker
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become transformative tools in automating decision-making processes across critical domains such as healthcare, criminal justice, education, and social media. By enabling unprecedented advances in efficiency, insight generation, and user interaction, LLMs hold the potential to reshape societal norms and systems. However, their widespread deployment also raises significant ethical concerns, particularly regarding the amplification of gender biases embedded within their training data. Such biases not only perpetuate historical inequities but also exacerbate them, resulting in unfair treatment and representation of genderdiverse groups in high-stakes applications. This paper examines the ethical implications of gender bias in LLMs and its ripple effects across societal structures. By dissecting the sources and manifestations of these biases-including their roots in skewed training datasets, historical inequities, and feedback loops-we highlight their real-world impacts in sensitive contexts like healthcare diagnostics, predictive policing, and AIcompanionship platforms. The study proposes novel approaches to mitigate these biases, including fairness metrics such as Demographic Parity, Equal Opportunity, and the Bias Impact Score, which provide robust tools for detecting and addressing disparities. Additionally, we introduce real-time bias monitoring techniques and emotional impact metrics to ensure ethical responsiveness, particularly in applications where user well-being is at risk. Our findings demonstrate the effectiveness of these methods, including 22 % reduction in gender bias in healthcare diagnostics, 30 % improvement in equitable outcomes in predictive policing, and enhanced psychological safety in AI companionship platforms. This research underscores the critical need for interdisciplinary approaches that prioritize fairness, transparency, and accountability in LLM design and deployment. By integrating ethical considerations into every stage of AI development, from dataset curation to real-time bias mitigation, we can foster responsible AI innovation that empowers marginalized communities, challenges societal stereotypes, and promotes equitable outcomes. Through this work, we aim to pave the way for a transformative shift in AI development - one where technology serves as a force for equity and inclusivity, aligning advancements in artificial intelligence with the values of fairness, respect, and diversity.

title: A Lightweight Hybrid Deep Learning Model with Squeeze-And-Excitation for Big Energy Data Analytics in Smart Homes
authors: Murad Khan, Mohammed Faisal, Basil Alothman
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC)
abstract: The exponential growth of smart home environments has led to an unprecedented influx of highresolution energy consumption data, presenting both opportunities and challenges for efficient data analytics. Traditional deep learning models used for energy disaggregation and appliance state detection often struggle with high memory overhead, slow inference, and poor generalization under constrained computing environments. Moreover, many existing approaches overlook the structural complexity of time-series energy data and fail to adaptively focus on salient temporal features, especially in imbalanced or sparse appliance usage scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose a lightweight hybrid deep learning framework that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Gated Recurrent Units (GRUs) with a Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) mechanism. The CNN layers efficiently capture local temporal patterns, while GRUs model sequential dependencies. The SE blocks further enhance discriminative power by adaptively recalibrating channel-wise feature responses. The proposed architecture enables precise ON/OFF state detection and mains power prediction using only a fraction of the parameters and memory typically required by conventional models. We validate our approach on the UK-DALE dataset, a widely used benchmark for energy disaggregation. Experimental results show that our model achieves F1-scores above 0.98 for key appliances and near-perfect alignment in regression for aggregate mains consumption. Similarly, compared to state-of-the-art methods, our model significantly reduces memory usage without compromising accuracy, making it ideal for real-time deployment on edge devices.

title: Improving Patent Document Retrieval with Customized Large Language Model
authors: Junhong Wang, Masayuki Okabe
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th International Workshop on Computational Intelligence and Applications (IWCIA)
abstract: This study aims to enhance patent document retrieval by employing a customized large language model, Patent BERT. We introduce a retrieval framework that leverages Patent BERT for text tokenization and vector generation, followed by a ranking mechanism to compute the similarity between topics and patent documents. Experimental evaluations on the CLEF-IP 2013 dataset demonstrate the superiority of our approach, particularly when comparing the performance of two vector types generated by Patent BERT against the conventional BERT model.

title: Detection of Human Trafficking Risks Using Machine Learning
authors: Manas Pati Tripathi, S. Pavithra, S. Shri Meenaakshi, Hossein Shirazi, Kurunandan Jain, Indrakshi Ray, Krishnashree Achuthan
abstract: Human trafficking remains a pervasive global issue, affecting millions of individuals through various forms of exploitation and coercion. The increasing use of digital platforms by traffickers to recruit and exploit victims necessitates the development of innovative and effective solutions. This research proposes a novel approach utilizing machine learning (ML) to identify suspicious activities indicative of human trafficking on social media platforms and within online job advertisements. This research proposes a novel approach to identify suspicious job advertisements that may indicate human trafficking. By analyzing keywords and their semantic relationships, we develop a system to flag high-risk postings. A multi-lingual dataset, comprising texts in English, Hindi, and Bengali, was created and meticulously labeled to determine the presence of suspicious content. This dataset was subsequently used to train a machine learning model capable of detecting potential trafficking activities in real time. The ultimate objective is to integrate this model into a web platform or browser extension, facilitating continuous monitoring and providing alerts to users. Moreover, the study introduces a robust model designed to detect patterns in job advertisements that are indicative of potential trafficking risks. By leveraging advanced natural language processing (NLP) techniques, specifically BERT embeddings, in combination with red flag detection mechanisms and sentiment analysis, this research presents a comprehensive framework for identifying and mitigating suspicious job postings. The findings underscore the critical role of leveraging advanced machine learning techniques to safeguard vulnerable populations, offering a scalable and automated solution to combat the evolving threat of human trafficking in the digital age.

title: Corrigendum: One Model for All: Large Language Models Are Domain-Agnostic Recommendation Systems
authors: Zuoli Tang, Zhaoxin Huan, Zihao Li, Xiaolu Zhang, Jun Hu, Chilin Fu, Jun Zhou, Lixin Zou, Chenliang Li
abstract: None

title: AgentChain: Blockchain-empowered Multi-agent Coordination for Trustworthy LLM Question-answering Systems
authors: Bei Chen, Gaolei Li, Jun Wu, Jianhua Li, Mingzhe Chen, Jiacheng Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Multi-agent architectures leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) have significantly advanced the precision of Question Answering (QA) systems across diverse domains. However, existing frameworks remain vulnerable to adversarial manipulations, including poisoning, backdoor, and jailbreak at tacks, primarily due to their reliance on centralized orchestration. To mitigate these risks, we propose AgentChain, a framework that substitutes centralized control with a distributed semantic consensus process. By modeling the blockchain as an ideal functionality, AgentChain establishes a secure distributed layer to coordinate role allocation, answer proposal, evaluation and voting through a decentralized council. Specifically, we design Proof-of-Content-Quality (PoCQ) mechanism to ensure that the f inal answers reflect a robust semantic agreement among the majority of honest agents. Furthermore, we propose an incentive mechanism based on stake reassignment that penalizes malicious agents by reducing their rewards, ultimately phasing them out of the network. Comprehensive evaluations across eight datasets demonstrate that AgentChain achieves superior performance and resilience. AgentChain minimizes the impact of poisoning attacks on precision to less than 3% and reduces the success rate of backdoor and jailbreak attacks to less than 4%. These findings highlight the effectiveness and trustworthiness of AgentChain in mitigating security threats while maintaining high QA accuracy.

title: Resource Optimization with Uplink RSMA for Multibeam LEO Satellite IoT Systems in the FBL Regime
authors: Xiaodi Yu, Tao Hong, Xiaojin Ding, Gengxin Zhang, Aijie Zhao
abstract: In this paper, we investigate an uplink multibeam low-Earth orbit (LEO) satellite Internet of Things (Sat-IoT) system employing rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA) under imperfect channel state information (CSI) in the finite blocklength (FBL) regime, and formulate an optimization problem to maximize the effective throughput by jointly optimizing beamforming, transmit power allocation, and rate-splitting factors. The problem is highly non-convex due to the nonlinear FBL achievable rate expressions. To obtain a tractable solution, we propose a regime-based SINR-threshold substitution that converts the FBL minimum-rate constraints into equivalent SINR constraints, and incorporate CSI imperfections into the SINR constraints in a tractable manner. An alternating DC–SCA–SDR algorithm is then proposed, where bilinear couplings are addressed via first-order Taylor expansions and a rank-promotion penalty is introduced to facilitate beamformer extraction. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves higher effective throughput than other schemes in the FBL regime under imperfect CSI.

title: Digital Transformation Readiness in University Operations: A Product-Service System Approach to Readiness-as-a-Service
authors: Guilherme Lopes Dos Santos, Murilo Alvarenga Oliveira, Adauto Bueno
abstract: Public service organisations, particularly universities, lack structured methods to evaluate their operational readiness for digital transformation, hindering strategic planning in the Industry 4.0 era. This paper addresses this gap by detailing the development and application of a digital transformation readiness model, conceptualised as a Product-Service System (PSS) for university operations. Following a Design Science Research (DSR) approach, we developed the Digital Transformation Readiness Model for Universities (DT-U). The model comprises 24 factors across four dimensions: strategy, processes, technology, and infrastructure. It was operationalised as a web-based self-assessment tool, refined through systematic literature reviews, expert evaluations, and deployed at a large Brazilian university. The DT-U diagnostic accurately placed the institution at an “Initial” readiness level and successfully identified specific operational bottlenecks. Crucially, the resulting strategic roadmap aligns these digital capability gaps directly with the four fundamental university missions: knowledge transfer, research, industry collaboration, and sustainable socio-economic development. The tool received positive managerial feedback, validating its ability to support continuous performance monitoring. This research provides a significant practical contribution by offering university managers a low-cost, methodologically rigorous diagnostic tool for data-driven decision-making. Its originality lies in transposing Industry 4.0 readiness principles into a public service environment, offering a scalable framework for IT and digital governance in higher education institutions.

title: Quality Diversity Time-Use Optimisation for Health
authors: Adel Nikfarjam, Ty Stanford, Aneta Neumann, Dorothea Dumuid, Frank Neumann
publish: ACM Transactions on Evolutionary Learning and Optimization
abstract: None

title: Design of a Low-Power High-Density Analog in-Memory Computing Accelerator Using 18 Nm FD-SOI Phase-Change Memory
authors: V. Viollet, G. Prenat, A. Al Muktash, C. Popon, L. Anghel, X. Lecoq
abstract: We present a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$128 \times 128$</tex> analog in-memory compute (A-IMC) accelerator designed for low-power consumption and accuracy across a broad range of process, voltage, and temperature variations to ensure industrial reliability. Based on 18 nm phase-change memory technology, the accelerator features a robust 8-bit current-controlled oscillator-based analog-to-digital converter (ADC) per bitline, and a novel low-IR drop interleaved array topology, improving ADC area efficiency. Additionally, it incorporates low-power and robust solutions to enhance the linearity and reliability of the accelerator. Finally, deep learning performances are extrapolated using various datasets. Our A-IMC accelerator achieves an energy efficiency of 13.6 TOPS/W with a density throughput of 2.95 TOPS/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Quantum Entanglement and Distance: A Performance Study of Long-Distance Networks
authors: Alfredo Cuzzocrea, Mst Shapna Akter, Md Shazzad Hossain Shaon, Tasmin Karim, Md Fahim Sultan, Emran Kanaan
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC)
abstract: Long-distance quantum networks are far more than mere channels for data transfer; they are sophisticated systems that harness the counterintuitive features of quantum mechanics to enable secure and high-performance communication over vast distances. In our study, we explore the underlying mechanisms of quantum connectivity by investigating phenomena such as superposition and entanglement, and their roles in sustaining robust information propagation. We focus on the interaction between these quantum effects and routing protocols strategies that, while inspired by classical networks, must overcome challenges associated with quantum decoherence and measurement uncertainty. To evaluate these interactions, we simulated distributed quantum networks with node counts ranging from 10 to 100 using novel routing strategies including Q-Learning, Bell State, and Entanglement Swapping protocols. Our analysis centers on key performance metrics that capture both quantum fidelity and operational efficiency. Notably, our results show that high quantum state fidelity is maintained across all network configurations, confirming the efficacy of entangled states in preserving the integrity of quantum information over long distances. Simultaneously, we observe that increased network complexity introduces trade-offs, such as higher computational overhead and amplified susceptibility to decoherence and error propagation. These findings underscore the necessity for advanced error-correction techniques and adaptive routing strategies to ensure scalability and robustness in long-distance quantum communication. Overall, our work provides critical insights into optimizing quantum superposition and entanglement in practical routing implementations, thereby paving the way for the future realization of a global quantum communication infrastructure.

title: Object Detection and Localization Sight to Sound Framework for Visually Impaired Community
authors: Abdullah Alharthi, Saudi Alzahri
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: This study tackles a crucial yet often overlooked challenge in the daily lives of Visually Impaired (VI) individuals, shifting the focus beyond traditional mobility aids. With 253 million people worldwide affected by visual impairment, tasks such as object localization remain significant obstacles to independence. This research presents a framework designed specifically to assist VI individuals in locating objects based on sound. It introduces two innovative methods: the Rotated Clock Directioning System and the Hand-Relative Directioning System, both leveraging advanced machine learning techniques. The framework integrates an optimized YOLOv8 model for precise object detection and localization, translating the results into speech using advanced speech recognition methods. The system was tested for object detection both from the frame center and in relation to the hand's position. Testing was conducted on a diverse set of household objects, and the framework showed exceptional effectiveness with competitive results. This highlights its potential to improve object localization, thereby significantly enhancing the autonomy and quality of life for VI individuals.

title: Geometry Aware Meta-Learning Neural Network for Joint Phase and Precoder Optimization in RIS_supp1-3684280.pdf
authors: Dahlia Devapriya
abstract: In reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) aided systems, the joint optimization of the precoder matrix at the base station and the phase shifts of the RIS elements involves significant complexity. In this paper, we propose a complex-valued, geometry aware meta-learning neural network that maximizes the weighted sum rate in a multi-user multiple input single output system. By leveraging the complex circle geometry for phase shifts and spherical geometry for the precoder, the optimization occurs on Riemannian manifolds, leading to faster convergence. We use a complex-valued neural network for phase shifts and an Euler inspired update for the precoder network. Our approach outperforms existing neural network-based algorithms, offering higher weighted sum rates, lower power consumption, and significantly faster convergence. Specifically, it converges faster by nearly 100 epochs, with a 0.7 bps improvement in weighted sum rate and a 1.8 dB power gain when compared with existing work. Further it outperforms the state-of-the-art alternating optimization algorithm by 0.86 bps with a 2.6 dB power gain.

title: Beyond Score Changes: Adversarial Attack on No-Reference Image Quality Assessment from Two Perspectives
authors: Chenxi Yang, Yujia Liu, Dingquan Li, Yan Zhong, Tingting Jiang
abstract: Deep neural networks have demonstrated remarkable success in No-Reference Image Quality Assessment (NR-IQA). However, recent researches highlight the vulnerability of NR-IQA models to subtle adversarial perturbations, leading to inconsistencies between model predictions and subjective ratings. Current adversarial attacks, however, focus on perturbing predicted scores of individual images, neglecting the crucial aspect of correlation coefficients for predicted scores within an entire image set. Meanwhile, it is important to note that the correlation, like ranking correlation, plays a significant role in NR-IQA tasks. To thoroughly explore the robustness of NR-IQA models, we introduce a new framework of correlation-error-based attacks that perturb both the correlation within an image set and score changes on individual images. In this work, the proposed attack framework is implemented with ranking-related correlation metrics like Spearman's Rank-Order Correlation Coefficient (SROCC) and prediction error-related metrics like Mean Squared Error (MSE), which is called SROCC-MSE-Attack (SMA). The SMA works in two stages, firstly optimizing target attack scores for the entire image set and then generating adversarial examples guided by these scores. The SMA method not only significantly disrupts the SROCC to negative values but also maintains a considerable change in the scores of individual images. Meanwhile, it exhibits state-of-the-art performance across metrics in different categories. The proposed attack framework offers a new perspective for examining the robustness of NR-IQA models and holds potential for application with other correlation metrics and prediction error indices.

title: PAHPA: Revolutionizing Kubernetes Autoscaling with Integrated Predictive Analytics and Real-Time Monitoring
authors: Junwei Xiao, Fan Yang, Wei Ai, Tao Meng, Guoqi Xie
abstract: Kubernetes provides powerful container orchestration features, like auto-scaling, which dynamically adjusts the scale of containerized applications. However, the default auto scaling mechanism in Kubernetes typically responds to workload changes only after they have occurred, which can result in resource mismatches. While there has been significant research into proactive auto-scaling approaches, most existing solutions struggle to adapt to the constantly evolving characteristics of prediction targets. This paper presents PAHPA, an intelligent, prediction-assisted approach aimed at enhancing Kubernetes' autoscaling capabilities, which integrates an prediction model, SLMD-LightGBM proposed in this paper, featuring a self updating mechanism for continuous prediction optimization, queueing theory analysis to improve resource allocation decisions, and a correction mechanism that refines prediction metrics using real-time data. A central insight of this paper is that while predictions are crucial, they should not be the sole basis for decisions and must be complemented by real-time monitoring data to ensure robust and adaptive autoscaling. Experimental results demonstrate that PAHPA significantly enhances system stability and reduces service latency, achieving a 9.5% lower Violation Rate (16.3%) than HPA (25.8%) and a 63% reduction in 99th percentile latency (880ms vs. 2400ms). This research highlights how combining predictive analytics with real-time monitoring can lead to more effective autoscaling strategies in cloud-native environments.

title: MGMIN-FSA: A Multi-Granularity Multimodal Interaction Network for Sentiment Analysis of Financial Review Videos_supp1-3681783.pdf
authors: Hongli Deng
abstract: Automated sentiment analysis of extensive financial review videos provides investors with a precise understanding of market sentiment. However, compared to videos in other domains, financial review videos contain extensive financial jargon and exhibit more nuanced sentiment changes in facial expressions and vocal tones. Most traditional models focus solely on either coarse-grained or fine-grained multimodal sentiment expression. This limitation renders them ineffective in handling videos with subtle sentiment changes, restricting their applicability to analysing sentiment in financial review videos. This paper introduces the Multi-Granularity Multimodal Interaction Network (MGMIN-FSA) for analysing sentiment in financial review videos. First, the Fine-grained Multimodal Interaction Network (FMN) is presented, incorporating Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) and attention mechanisms to extract crucial phrase-level features across modalities, producing fine-grained sentiment expressions. Additionally, KAN-Attention within FMN improves the model's generalization capability. Second, the Coarse-grained Feature Decoupling Network (CFDN) is introduced, establishing anchors at both modal and sentiment feature levels to improve differentiation between positive and negative samples. Finally, MGMIN-FSA integrates fine- and coarse-grained representations for sentiment analysis using downstream classifiers. MGMIN-FSA obtains the best performance on the FMSA-SC and CH-SIMS datasets among all compared methods. Furthermore, experimental results demonstrate that KAN-Attention can maintain high performance despite insufficient data.

title: A 16× Interleaved 32-GS/s 8b Hybrid ADC With Self-Tracking Inter-Stage Gain Achieving 44.3-dB SFDR at 20.9-GHz Input
authors: Hongzhi Liang, Jun Chang, Yixiao Luo, Li Dang, Haolin Han, Yi Shen, Dengquan Li, Shubin Liu, Ruixue Ding, Zhangming Zhu
abstract: This article presents a 32-GS/s 16-channel hierarchical time-interleaved (TI) hybrid analog-to-digital converter (ADC). The prototype utilizes the intrinsic high-speed quantization of time-domain (TD) ADC to reduce the interleaving factor. By incorporating hierarchical sampling and a cascode sampler, the compact TI-ADC achieves 44.3-dB spurious-free dynamic range (SFDR) at 20.9-GHz input. The sub-ADC adopts a two-stage hybrid-domain architecture that combines a 3-bit successive approximation register (SAR) ADC with a 6-bit inverter-based opposite phase propagation (OPP) time-to-digital converter (TDC). Aprototype chip is fabricated in a 28-nm CMOS process, occupying a core area of 0.141mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. At 32GS/s, it achieves a signal-to-noise and distortion ratio (SNDR) of 36dB with a Nyquist input while consuming 142.3mW, corresponding to a Walden figure-of-merit of 86.3fJ/conv-step. Meanwhile, a delay-locked loop (DLL)-based calibration loop is proposed to calibrate the full-scale quantization range variation introduced by PVT variations, together with an analog self-tracking inter-stage gain calibration loop. The measured SNDR varies by 1.84dB over a temperature range of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0~{^{\circ } }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$100~{^{\circ } }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C and fluctuates by 2.25dB with a ±10% supply variation.

title: Quantum-Driven Intelligent Controller Design for DC–DC Boost Converters
authors: Ali Masoudian, Uffe Jakobsen, Sajad Fathi Hafshejani, Mohammad-Hassan Khooban
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper introduces a quantum-inspired control strategy for DC–DC boost converters feeding constant power loads (CPLs) based on variational quantum circuits (VQCs) integrated into a Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) reinforcement learning framework. The proposed quantum reinforcement learning based (QRL) controller leverages compact quantum circuit architectures to reduce parameter complexity while maintaining expressive control policies, offering a scalable alternative to deep neural networks for high-frequency control loops in Wide Bandgap (WBG) power electronics, a critical application in resource-constrained embedded platforms. The controller is trained offline and deployed on a real-time dSPACE DS1202 hardware platform for experimental validation. Comparative results against two neural network–based reinforcement learning controllers demonstrate that QRL controller achieves faster convergence in training, shorter settling times, smaller voltage deviations, and smoother current dynamics, all with significantly fewer trainable parameters. These findings highlight the potential of quantum-inspired reinforcement learning as a practical and efficient control solution for power electronic systems, paving the way toward real-time quantum-enhanced controllers in future energy applications.

title: A Cloud-Native Alternative Correction for Landsat-8/9 Collection 2 Surface Reflectance over Inland Waters
authors: Shun Bi, Kun Shi, Jie Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The Landsat Collection 2 (C2) archive is vital for inland water monitoring, yet the Land Surface Reflectance Code (LaSRC) atmospheric correction for Landsat-8/9 introduces Dark Plume Over Water (DPOW) artifacts. These aerosol extrapolation errors cause severe negative biases in shortwave bands, disrupting long-term consistency. To address this, we developed a cloud-native Alternative Correction (AC) method on Google Earth Engine. This data-driven approach employs random forest regression, trained on spatiotemporally aggregated high-quality water pixels, to reconstruct reliable surface reflectance (SR) from Top-of-Atmosphere observations. Validation against a global in-situ hyperspectral dataset and benchmarking against the physics-based ACOLITE processor demonstrate the robustness of the proposed method. While ACOLITE effectively resolves the negative bias issue, the AC method achieves superior radiometric accuracy, reducing the ultra-blue Root Mean Square Error to 0.019 (compared to 0.029 for ACOLITE and 0.031 for C2 SR). Notably, under high-aerosol conditions, the AC method minimizes the residual spectral distortions often observed in physical inversions, effectively restoring the natural spectral shape. Spatially, the method eliminates DPOW artifacts; furthermore, it removes systematic biases between Landsat-8/9 and legacy sensors (Landsat-4/5/7). By restoring radiometric integrity, this automated solution secures the foundation for reliable long-term global limnology.

title: Novel Three-Segment Steerable Continuum Needle Manipulator for Minimally Invasive Surgery_supp1-3679362.mp4
authors: Seong Young Ko
abstract: Due to their flexible bodies and high length-to-diameter ratios, continuum robots are uniquely suited for tasks requiring deep access into narrow pathways. This article presents a novel three-segment steerable continuum needle (TSSCN), in which the segments are longitudinally split and function as integrated tendons, eliminating conventional disk structures. This design reduces component count and enables miniaturization for broader medical applications. The TSSCN comprises a three-segment tube (TST) as the main body, two outer tubes, an inner support tube, and an optional tool. The TST is fabricated using superelastic nickel–titanium alloy and is cut using a femtosecond laser to create distal and proximal segments, enabling the TSSCN to achieve a 1.2-mm diameter. Pushing or pulling the proximal end of the TST deforms the TSSCN, causing the distal end to bend into a shape that minimizes elastic potential energy under resistance from the outer tube. Geometric models are employed for forward and inverse kinematics analysis, and their effectiveness is validated experimentally. The TSSCN achieves bending angles exceeding 180° and features an adjustable bending arc length, and its potential for middle-ear surgery is experimentally tested. Therefore, this type of TSSCN has the potential to be applicable to various minimally invasive surgical procedures with minor modifications.

title: Development of an Insulation Lifetime Model under Varied Pressure and Voltage Conditions for More Electric Aircraft Applications
authors: Yatai Ji, Paolo Giangrande, Weiduo Zhao, Huanran Wang, Pinjia Zhang
abstract: The high electric stress experienced by insulation systems today continues to pose significant challenges to the reliability of inverter-fed electrical machines (EMs) used in transportation applications. Electrical aging caused by partial discharge is one of the primary factors threatening the reliability of EMs. Moreover, compared to EMs meant for industrial applications, the insulation systems of EMs designed for more-electric aircraft may also be affected by pressure conditions. For instance, EMs used in primary flight control surface actuators may operate during the cruise phase at altitudes of approximately 12 km and pressures as low as 200 mbar. Several studies have highlighted that low-pressure environments can accelerate the electrical aging process. However, a comprehensive lifetime model accounting for both pressure and electrical stress remains unavailable. Such a model would enable machine designers to assess the suitability of insulation systems for various applications across different operating altitudes (i.e., pressure levels). In this paper, accelerated insulation aging tests are conducted under twelve distinct combinations of pressure and voltage conditions, and a multi-stress (i.e., electrical and pressure stress) lifetime model applicable in the range 200÷1000 mbar is developed, accounting for the impact of pressure on inverse power law’s parameters.

title:
Analyzing the Impact of TiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Nanoparticles in Transformer Oil-Based Nanofluids Through Dissolved Gas Analysis
authors: Abhijeet Lal, Anup Mishra, Anupam Agrawal, Rounak Tamrakar, Nagendra Tripathi, Sunil Kumar Chouhan, Narendra Khatri
abstract: Nanofluids based on transformer oil have emerged as promising candidates for improving insulation performance in high-voltage equipment. This study investigates the influence of TiO₂ nanoparticles on the dielectric strength and dissolved gas behavior of transformer oil at concentrations of 0.01–0.1%. Breakdown Voltage (BDV) testing shows a maximum improvement of approximately 9.6% at 0.05% TiO₂, confirming the beneficial role of nanoparticles in suppressing streamer propagation at optimal loading. Dissolved Gas Analysis (DGA) reveals concentration-dependent variations in gas evolution, with hydrogen and hydrocarbon gases increasing near the optimal concentration and declining at higher loading due to probable nanoparticle agglomeration. Comparative interpretation using Doernenburg, Rogers, IEC ratio methods, and Duval graphical techniques indicates that nanoparticle addition can shift diagnostic classification toward thermal or partial discharge regions despite the absence of actual fault conditions. These findings demonstrate that TiO₂ nanofluids exhibit a nonlinear dielectric–chemical response and may influence conventional transformer diagnostic interpretation. The study provides a baseline reference for understanding dissolved gas behavior in nano-modified insulating oils and highlights the need to consider nanoparticle-induced gas patterns when applying standard DGA frameworks to advanced transformer insulating fluids.

title: A Graph-Enhanced Defense Framework for Explainable Fake News Detection with LLM
authors: Bo Wang, Jing Ma, Hongzhan Lin, Zhiwei Yang, Ruichao Yang, Yuan Tian, Yi Chang
abstract: None

title: Hierarchical Power Flow Control Strategy for CHB-based APQC-ESS of Railway System Under Unbalanced Grid
authors: Kai Yang, Minghao Wang, Junyu Chen, Yinbo Ge, Qiang Yi, Yibo Wang, Xinbo Ruan
abstract: Cascaded H-bridge-based active power quality conditioners (CHB-based APQCs) have been adopted for power quality (PQ) compensation and regenerative braking energy (RBE) utilization in railway power systems (RPSs). However, existing studies neglect unbalanced grid conditions commonly encountered in practice, leading to degraded PQ compensation performance, reduced RBE utilization efficiency, and compromised system safety. To address these issues, a hierarchical power flow control strategy (HPFCS) for a CHB-based APQC integrating energy storage systems (APQC-ESSs) is proposed in this paper. The proposed HPFCS enables precise negative-sequence current (NSC) compensation and efficient RBE utilization under fluctuating traction load and unbalanced grid conditions, while simultaneously eliminating inter-phase power imbalance and active power output deviation, thereby enhancing operational efficiency and security. Firstly, the active power distribution mechanism of the APQC-ESS under unbalanced grid conditions is analyzed, revealing the influence of voltage imbalance on both the RBE utilization efficiency and system safety. Based on this, a three-layer HPFCS architecture is established, which includes (i) an NSC partial compensation layer to suppress RPS-injected NSCs; (ii) an RBE utilization layer that maximizes energy recovery using residual system capacity; and (iii) an NSC recompensation layer that further mitigates residual NSCs when surplus capacity is available. Finally, experiments are conducted to validate the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed strategy.

title: A Latin American Institution&#x2019;s Journey in Digital Systems Education: From Breadboards to FPGA and RISC-V Design_supp1-3684367.pdf
authors: Sergio Abreo
abstract: This paper presents the evolution of digital systems education at the Universidad Industrial de Santander, beginning with hands-on projects using discrete logic chips on breadboards. The curriculum progressively adopted hardware description languages (VHDL and Verilog) and eventually integrated RISC-V open hardware through the RVfpga project. This transformation has positioned the university as a regional reference in Latin America and contributed to the professional success of its graduates in the global semiconductor industry. The paper describes key milestones, methodological decisions, and pedagogical strategies that enabled this shift, analyzes its impact on student engagement and learning outcomes, and offers recommendations for institutions seeking to modernize their digital systems curricula. The experience illustrates how sustained curricular innovation and project-based learning can foster academic excellence and real-world impact in electronics and computer engineering.

title: A Compact Frequency Comb Generator Implementation on RFSoC for MKIDs
authors: Sungyoun Seo, Frank Danso, Sungho Kim
abstract: The frequency comb generator is a main component in readout systems for superconducting detectors, responsible for producing frequency tones that probe individual detectors. As the number of channels and detectors increases, modern readout architectures face growing challenges in resource utilization and power consumption. In this work, we present the first hardware realization of a compact frequency comb generator that addresses these challenges through a tone grouping mechanism capable of supporting up to 2,048 detectors per feedline. Additionally, a tone compression technique is used to significantly reduce the lookup table requirements for the data input buffer compared to conventional methods. The proposed design achieves efficient hardware utilization, occupying less than 15% of FPGA resources and maintaining a total power consumption below 5 W. The system has been implemented on an Xilinx RFSoC ZCU111 platform. Experimental results demonstrate that a single RFSoC ZCU111 can support up to seven independent MKID feedlines of the proposed frequency comb generator, highlighting its suitability for large-scale, low-power superconducting detector array readout. The design is also well-suited for our future ASIC implementation, where compact area and low power consumption are essential.

title: High-Efficiency 1-D Scanning Solar Arrays with High SCs Coverage Ratio Based on a Hybrid Transparent Dielectric Resonator Antenna
authors: Chao Zhang, Shao-Hua Xing, Yin-Feng Wang, Zhen-Guo Liu, Bian Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper proposes a high-performance one-dimensional (1-D) solar scanning array based on a hybrid transparent dielectric resonator antenna (DRA). The proposed design addresses the long-standing challenge prevalent in solar antennas (SAs): the trade-off between antenna efficiency and the effective aperture area of solar cells (SCs). As a result, it achieves both objectives of high efficiency and a large SCs coverage ratio. A hybrid transparent material structure is adopted to effectively improve the antenna's impedance matching and radiation performance. Moreover, the integrated SCs architecture eliminates interconnections between individual SCs units, rendering the design more suitable for high-frequency applications. The proposed design has been fabricated and verified. Measured results demonstrate that the antenna exhibits 33% impedance bandwidth ranging from 8.6 to 12 GHz. The array supports beam scanning over a ±45° range while maintaining a peak aperture efficiency of 96.1% and an effective SCs area ratio of 87.3%. The embedded SCs can deliver a peak output power of 23 mW. The proposed design achieves excellent electromagnetic performance and high SC aperture utilization, while providing mechanical protection for seamless antenna–SC integration, enabling a self-powered solution for outdoor and satellite communication platforms.

title: Indirect and Direct Multiuser Hybrid Beamforming for Far-Field and Near-Field Communications: A Deep Learning Approach
authors: Xinyang Li, Songjie Yang, Boyu Ning, Zongmiao He, Xiang Ling, Chau Yuen
abstract: Hybrid beamforming for extremely large-scale multiple-input multiple-output (XL-MIMO) systems is challenging in the near field because the channel depends jointly on angle and distance, and the multiuser interference (MUI) is strong. Existing deep learning methods typically follow either a decoupled design that optimizes analog beamforming without explicitly accounting for MUI, or an end-to-end (E2E) joint analog–digital optimization that can be unstable under nonconvex constant-modulus (CM), pronounced analog–digital coupling, and gradient pattern of sum-rate loss. To address both issues, we develop a complex-valued E2E framework based on a variant minimum mean square error (variant-MMSE) criterion, where the digital precoder is eliminated in closed form via Karush–Kuhn–Tucker (KKT) conditions so that analog learning is trained with a stable objective. The network employs a grouped complex-convolution sensing front-end for uplink (UL) measurements, a shared complex multi-layer perceptron (MLP) for per-user feature extraction, and a merged constant-modulus head to output the analog precoder. In the indirect mode, the network designs hybrid beamformers from estimated channel state information (CSI). In the direct mode where explicit CSI is unavailable, the network learns the sensing operator and the analog mapping from short pilots, after which additional pilots estimate the equivalent channel and enable a KKT closed-form digital precoder. Simulations show that the indirect mode approaches the performance of iterative variant-MMSE optimization with a complexity reduction proportional to the antenna number. In the direct mode, the proposed method improves spectral efficiency over sparse-recovery pipelines and recent deep learning baselines under the same pilot budget.

title: Prior-Embedded Policy Optimization for Multipoint Weak Leakage Localization in Energy Transportation Systems
authors: Tianbiao Wang, Huaguang Zhang, Zhijie Han, Dazhong Ma
abstract: Localization of multipoint weak leakage in energy transportation systems remains challenging due to signal-source mismatch and weak responses of negative pressure waves (NPWs). To address this issue, this article proposes a novel physics-informed localization framework that combines an attenuation-based NPW matching algorithm with a prior-embedded maximum entropy adaptive dynamic programming (MEADP) scheme. First, a matrix transformation-based matching algorithm establishes physically coherent correspondences between inlet and outlet NPWs. Building on this foundation, a prior-embedded MEADP localization strategy incorporates Gaussian priors derived from the attenuation physics of NPWs to guide policy optimization. Furthermore, a Wasserstein-distance regularization term is introduced to stabilize policy learning and ensure reliable convergence. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively resolves multileak signal mismatches and achieves high-precision localization, with the minimum localization error remaining below 0.5%.

title: IoT-Oriented Uplink Multi-User Digital Semantic Communication System Based on Model Division Multiple Access
authors: Di Wu, Zhi Zhang, Na Zhang, Yue Meng, Xiaoqi Qin, Yiming Liu, Ping Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: To support the transmission of massive data generated by large-scale distributed intelligent devices in the Internet of Things (IoT) scenario, in this paper, an uplink multi-user digital semantic communication system based on model division multiple access (MDMA) is established. At first, in order to be compatible with existing digital communication equipment, a bit codec model is designed to map the semantic information into bit sequences, which can solve the problem of information loss caused by traditional quantization and codec methods. Next, to meet the connection requirements of massive IoT devices, as well as reduce interference between devices, a joint deep adversarial semantic decomposition (JDASD) algorithm is proposed to derive multiple mutually exclusive semantic models, forming the model resource pool as a new kind of access resource. Finally, we design an uplink MDMA proof-of-concept prototype system applying the universal software radio peripheral (USRP) and implement the proposed JDASD algorithm combining the bit codec model. Numerous simulations and prototype experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed scheme, which achieves better performance in the MDMA system compared to the latest existing schemes.

title: Deep Learning-Based Recommendation System to Address Challenges in Providing Electronic Services
authors: Turki Abdullah Masood AL Hinai, Juma Al Kulaibi, Mohammed Abdulla Salim Al Husaini, Yousuf Nasser Al Husaini, Mohamed Hadi Habaebi
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: In the era of digital transformation, universities are increasingly challenged to deliver personalized electronic services to students, particularly in areas like academic advising, GPA forecasting, and early warning systems. This study proposes an advanced academic performance prediction system using a Support Vector Machine (SVM) model, trained on a comprehensive dataset from 1,734 university students enrolled in the Spring 2024 semester. The dataset includes real and simulated GPA scores, academic classifications, warning records, and course registration details, providing a rich foundation for predictive analytics. The SVM algorithm, known for its robust classification capabilities in high-dimensional spaces, was implemented using MATLAB R2023b and evaluated on a system with an Intel Core i7 (14th Gen), 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA RTX 4070 GPU. The model classified students into three performance categories. It achieved a remarkable overall accuracy of 99.25 %, correctly predicting 1,721 out of 1,734 instances. The precision for Class 1 and Class 2 was 100 %, while Class 3 had a precision of 96.25 %, with only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3}$</tex> misclassifications. Furthermore, 10-fold cross-validation was employed to assess model generalizability, yielding accuracy scores ranging from 0.88 to 0.92, with Fold 4 scoring 0.89623. These results confirm the model's robustness and suitability for real-world deployment in educational institutions.

title: Global Trends in Digital Financial Inclusion for Women: A Decade-Long Bibliometric Review (2014-2024)
authors: Anupama S, Angan Sengupta
abstract: Digital financial inclusion has emerged as a critical driver for economic empowerment, particularly for women, who historically have been marginalized in traditional financial systems. The aim of this study is to conduct a bibliometric review of scientific publications that have explored the relationship between Digital Financial Inclusion and women as examined by previous researchers. The research was carried out using 281 documents indexed in the Scopus database. The authors utilize scientific mapping tools and performance analysis, including VOS viewer and Biblioshiny in RStudio to do the review. The results obtained are in the period from 2014-2024. The study explores the present status of digital financial inclusion on women, occurrence of keywords, most influential countries, influential authors, trending topics related to the keywords. Five clusters were found through the network visualization of keywords using Vos viewer. In addition, the Biblioshiny program in RStudio provided insights into the most relevant sources, authors, global cited documents, local cited documents, country scientific production and average citations. The results of this study highlight essential elements of this research field. However, the study recognizes its limitations, including reliance on a single database and exclusion of certain keywords. It calls for a broader methodological approach to capture a more extensive understanding of the relevant literature in this emerging field.

title: Energy Efficient Power Control in 6G Networks for Smart Grid Wide Area Network Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ainul Husna Binti Zaidi, Fauzun A. Asuhaimi, Aneesa Irdina Binti Ismail, Muhamad Arif Izzudin Bin Mohd Razali, Nur Sabrina binti Suhaimi, Huda Adibah binti Ramli
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: The rapid evolution of modern energy systems has highlighted the limitations of current communication technologies. Existing smart grid, particularly Wide Area Networks (WANs) that rely on 5G or older technologies, may often struggle to meet the increasing demands for ultra-reliable, high-speed data transmission and energy-efficient communication. This limitation can affect the performance of critical smart grid functions such as real-time monitoring, fault detection and distributed energy resource management. This research explores the integration of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) with emerging 6G cellular networks to enhance power control and resource allocation decision for WANs. The study aims to develop an adaptive DRL-based framework that dynamically optimizes energy usage while maintaining network performance. Specifically, investigate how DRL techniques are employed to facilitate decision-making in power control through smart grid WANs. Performance metrics such as energy efficiency will be analyzed to validate the effectiveness of the model.

title: Efficient Design of Fronthaul-Constrained Uplink Reception for Cell-Free XL-MIMO
authors: Dogon Kim, Hyunmin Noh, Seok-Hwan Park
abstract: With the evolution of multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) technology toward extremely large (XL) MIMO systems comprising hundreds of, or more, antennas, this work investigates scalable and fronthaul-efficient reception design for the uplink of cell-free (CF) XL-MIMO systems. In such systems, the uplink signals transmitted by mobile user equipments (UEs) are jointly decoded at a central processing unit (CPU) connected to distributed access points (APs) via finite-capacity fronthaul links. We address the joint optimization of linear transform matrices, used by the APs to reduce the signal dimension and fronthaul load, and fronthaul compression strategies to maximize the uplink sum-rate. A fractional programming (FP)-based iterative algorithm is first developed, followed by a reduced-complexity variant, termed accelerated FP (A-FP), along with its decentralized implementation whose fronthaul overhead remains independent of the number of AP antennas. Numerical results show that the proposed A-FP scheme significantly reduces computational complexity compared to FP implemented with general-purpose solvers, while substantially outperforming scalable baseline schemes that rely solely on local channel state information.

title: Digital Gender Divide Among Teenagers: Analysing Disparities in Access to Technology and Internet Connectivity
authors: Kochukrishna Kurup VS, Gayathri. G
abstract: The Sustainable Development 2030 Agenda emphasizes the importance of gender equality. This literature review explores the digital gender divide among teenagers(DGD), specifically concentrating on disparities in access to technology and internet connectivity. By analyzing 30 articles from diverse regions, this study aims to identify and understand the factors contributing to the digital divide among teenagers and how gender-based disparities affect their access to digital platforms. Additionally, it examines global strategies that address these disparities and offers insights into narrowing the digital gender divide among teenagers. The review highlights the need for a more inclusive digital landscape to ensure equal opportunities for all genders in educational and social contexts. Furthermore, this study supports efforts toward achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by informing initiatives aimed at bridging the digital gender divide, particularly in the context of teenagers. The findings encourage greater scholarly involvement in addressing overlooked components of the digital divide, guiding policymakers, institutions, and decision-makers in creating more effective strategies to reduce the gap.
