Despite a load current total harmonic distortion (THD) of 29.61%, the AQ-LMF control limits supply current THD to 4.11%, complying with the IEEE-519 (<5%) standard. Experimental validation under dynamic conditions confirms the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed approach for reliable standalone MG operation.

title: Heterogeneous Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning for Edge Intelligence
authors: Zhuangzhuang Zhang, Libing Wu, Helei Cui, Zhibo Wang, Lijuan Huo, Jing Wang, Shengshan Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Federated learning (FL) as a distributed machine learning paradigm can be applied to edge intelligence scenarios for collaborative machine learning model building. Unfortunately, existing privacy-preserving FL applied to this scenario still faces three challenges: data heterogeneity, model heterogeneity, and privacy heterogeneity. Despite numerous privacy-preserving FL techniques proposed, they still cannot effectively address these three challenges. To solve this problem, we propose HeteroFed, a heterogeneous privacy-preserving FL framework for edge intelligence. Our HeteroFed contains heterogeneous model construction, dynamic gradient clipping, adaptive noise addition, and deviation-aware model aggregation. Specifically, we first use the heterogeneous model construction mechanism to enable personalized model training for different smart devices. Then, we propose a dynamic gradient clipping mechanism to perform dynamically adjusted gradient clipping on models uploaded by smart devices to limit the magnitude of gradients. Finally, we propose an adaptive noise addition mechanism to customize differential privacy protection for smart device models based on their convergence status. Furthermore, to mitigate the influence of noise perturbations on model performance, we propose a deviation-aware model aggregation mechanism for accurate model aggregation. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that HeteroFed achieves heterogeneous differential privacy. Extensive experiments show that HeteroFed outperforms similar methods, improving global model accuracy by 18%, 15%, 13%, and 18% on the MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10, and THUCNews datasets, respectively.

title: Toward Multi-Dimensional Quality Assessment of AI-Generated Images via Prompt-Guided Relational Learning
authors: Hangwei Chen, Feng Shao, Weiyi Jing, Xiaolong Tang, Xiongli Chai, Qiuping Jiang
abstract: The explosive growth of Artificial Intelligence Generated Images (AGIs) has created an urgent need for automated quality assessment. Unlike traditional image quality assessment, AGIs evaluation faces core challenges such as the absence of reference images, the need to integrate textual information, and the multi-dimensional nature of evaluation criteria. Existing methods typically rely on shared multimodal features for unified modeling, failing to effectively address two key issues: the differentiated extraction of dimension-specific features and the modeling of intrinsic relationships between dimensions. To address this, this paper proposes a Prompt-guided Relationship Learning Network (PRL-Net). Specifically, we first design a dimension-specific prompt attention (DPA) module, which introduces learnable prompts for each quality dimension to guide the model in adaptively focusing on dimension-related feature representations from shared features. Furthermore, we introduce a cross-dimensional graph relational attention (CGRA) module that explicitly models the complex dependencies between different quality dimensions (such as quality, consistency, and authenticity) through a dynamic graph neural network, enabling cross-dimensional feature interaction and information complementarity. Experimental results on multiple public benchmark datasets demonstrate that PRL-Net outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods across several evaluation metrics, validating its effectiveness.

title: Exploring MLLMs Perception of Network Visualization Principles_supp1-3681983.pdf
authors: Jacob Miller

title: Reduced Maximum Curvature-Based Linear Path Smoothing Method with Enhanced Kinematic Performance for 6R Robot Manipulators
authors: Yan Xu, Yaqiu Liu, Xun Liu, Jiabin Cao, Lina Liu, Peixing Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: To address dynamic instability, efficiency loss, and precision degradation from tangential and normal discontinui-ties in linear paths, a C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>-continuous local smoothing method is proposed for microscale, high-precision 6R robot trajectories. With maximum curvature control and analytical error control as the primary objective, an analytical smoothing framework based on “outward-extended control point + error tube con-straint” is established. Rather than confining splines to corners, outward-extended control points are analytically optimized under internal and external error constraints to reduce maxi-mum curvature, suppress curvature peaks and enhance conti-nuity and execution smoothness. To ensure global continuity, a C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>-continuous path structure sharing the same parameter is constructed to synchronize position and orientation. For 6R robot with highly nonlinear kinematics, an enhanced orientation spline strategy and a consistent analytical model of internal and external orientation errors are developed to ensure robustness under complex joint constraints. A unified path-classification framework is also introduced to meet varying requirements in curvature control and execution stability. Sim-ulations and experiments demonstrate that, for 6R robots with highly nonlinear kinematics, the proposed method achieves curvature reduction, orientation smoothness, and joint motion stability while satisfying both geometric and kinematic con-straints of the robot’s joints.

title: Reducing annotation effort in patient-ventilator asynchrony detection with distance-based clustering
authors: Lars van de Kamp, Dolf Weller, Rick Thijssen, Bram Hunnekens, Tom Bakkes, Simona Turco, Corstiaan den Uil, Tom Oomen, Nathan van de Wouw
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Control Systems
abstract: Objective: This study aims to reduce expert annotation effort in detecting patient-ventilator asynchrony (PVA) by introducing a semi-supervised learning framework for time series classification. Methods and procedures: We propose a model-independent framework that integrates hierarchical clustering and dynamic time-warping (DTW) for efficient data selection and label projection. The framework includes five steps: data collection, selection, annotation, projection, and model training. It is validated using a fully labeled dataset from Fondazione I.R.C.C.S. Policlinico San Matteo and applied to an unlabeled dataset from Maasstad Hospital, where annotation consistency and label quality are analyzed. Results: The framework reduces annotation effort by over 75% while closely resembling classification performance. On the San Matteo dataset, the model trained with projected labels achieved performance close to that of a fully supervised model. The method effectively captured rare PVA types and improved macro-averaged F1 scores compared to random sampling. On the Maasstad dataset, despite annotation inconsistencies, the framework demonstrated moderate detection performance (75% micro-averaged <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$F_{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula> score) using labels from a single clinical expert. Conclusion: Our semi-supervised framework enables scalable and efficient annotation of clinical time series data, maintaining model accuracy with minimal expert input. It is robust across datasets and adaptable to varying signal quality and annotation consistency.

title: Design and Analysis of a Novel In-Wheel Machine-Based Powertrain for HTS Maglev Systems
authors: Zhentao Ding, Jingguo Bi, Wangxuan Lin, Rui Zhao, Changhao Hu, Maoru Chi, Zigang Deng
abstract: High-temperature superconducting (HTS) maglev systems, as promising transportation solutions, have garnered growing attention in maglev transportation due to their inherent passive self-stability. To leverage this advantage, this paper pro-poses a compact propulsion system featuring a contactless power-train with a permanent magnet electrodynamic in-wheel motor (PMEIM). First, analytical modelling is employed to characterize the external magnetic field of the PMEIM, and its characteristics under multiple operating conditions are derived via coordinate transformation. Subsequently, the eddy current model of the secondary conductor plate is established, and its reflected mag-netic field is solved. Based on this, the electromagnetic force model of the PMEIM powertrain is developed in accordance with the magnetic charge theory, and the corresponding mechanical characteristics are determined. Next, the PMEIM design is con-ducted, with its load characteristics and transient response eval-uated by electromagnetic analysis and co-simulation. Finally, a small-scale prototype is fabricated and tested. Results indicate that theoretical predictions are in close agreement with the ex-perimental measurements, with the measured propulsion and guidance forces being 12.70 N and 41.14 N, respectively, under rated operating conditions. The integrated PMEIM configuration enables contactless force and torque transmission, demonstrating potential as a compact, flexible, and cost-effective propulsion solution for HTS maglev systems.

title: Beam Squint Mitigation in Wideband Hybrid Beamformers: Full-TTD, Sparse-TTD, or Non-TTD?
authors: Mehdi Monemi, Mohammad Amir Fallah, Mehdi Rasti, Omid Yazdani, Onel L. A. Lopez, Matti Latva-aho
abstract: Beam squint poses a fundamental challenge in wideband hybrid beamforming, particularly for mmWave and THz systems that demand both ultra-wide bandwidth and high directional beams. While conventional phase shifter-based beamformers may offer partial mitigation, True Time Delay (TTD) units provide a fundamentally more effective solution by enabling frequency-independent beam steering. However, the high cost of TTD units has recently driven much interest in Sparse-TTD architectures, which combine a limited number of TTDs with a higher number of conventional PSs to balance performance and cost. This paper provides a critical examination of beam squint mitigation strategies in wideband hybrid beamformers, comparing Full-TTD, Sparse-TTD, and Non-TTD architectures. We analyze recent Non-TTD approaches, specifically the scheme leveraging the wideband beam gain (WBBG) concept, evaluating their performance and cost characteristics against TTD-based solutions. A key focus is placed on the practical limitations of Sparse-TTD architectures, particularly the often-overlooked requirement for wideband PSs operating alongside TTDs, which can significantly impact performance and implementation cost in real-world scenarios, especially for ultra-wideband applications. Finally, we conduct a cost-performance analysis to examine the trade-offs inherent in each architecture and provide guidance on selecting the most suitable hybrid beamforming structure for various fractional bandwidth regimes.

title: Design and Performance of a 10 GHz HTS He-FIB JJ Mixer on LSAT Substrate
authors: Timothy Cosgrove, Max Pröpper, Christoph Schmid, Edward Goldobin, Dieter Koelle, Reinhold Kleiner, Meinhard Schilling, Benedikt Hampel, Richard Taylor
abstract: This paper presents a compact, single-ended high-temperature superconducting (HTS) helium-focused ion beam (He-FIB) Josephson junction (JJ) mixer. The planar circuit is fabricated from a thin film layer of YBa<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>Cu<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7-x</sub>  deposited on a 10 mm × 10 mm Lanthanum Strontium Aluminium Tantalate (LSAT) substrate. A comprehensive six-step microstrip design methodology is outlined, detailing the JJ's physical geometries, high frequency and DC grounding, impedance matching, and filtering networks. The complete microstrip topology is designed to achieve an approximate radio frequency (RF) input passband between 9.9 GHz to 10.6 GHz with high out-of-band isolation, a single local oscillator (LO) resonance at 11.54 GHz and an intermediate frequency (IF) lowpass cut-off at 2.3 GHz. The proposed RF impedance matching network is uniquely designed to mitigate the significant performance limitation associated with normal resistances (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\rm{R}_\rm{n}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) scatter observed in HTS JJs. By implementing the proposed design methodology, a stable percentage bandwidth and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\rm{S}_{11}$</tex-math></inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\leq$</tex-math></inline-formula> -10  dB is maintained between <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\mathit{R}}_\rm{n} =$</tex-math></inline-formula> 5 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega$</tex-math></inline-formula> to 10 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega$</tex-math></inline-formula>, which coincides with the fabrication variance for He-FIB JJs. The HTS mixer is manufactured and its DC and high frequency behaviours are characterised at 6  K, 10  K, 20  K, 30  K, and 40  K. The measured <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\mathit{R}}_\rm{n}$</tex-math></inline-formula> is 9.77 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega$</tex-math></inline-formula>, which significantly deviated from the predicted value of 5 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega$</tex-math></inline-formula>, emphasising the importance of the adaptability and versatility of the implemented impedance matching network. Exposed to the lowest measured temperature, the HTS mixer exhibited a maximum conversion gain of -4.93  dB which demonstrates the potential capabilities in He-FIB induced barrier JJs on LSAT substrates as frequency mixers.

title: eXAI-CodeDebug: Towards Reliable, Trustworthy, and Explainable LLM-Based Coding for Production Systems
authors: Srishti Dubey, Vijay K. Madisetti
abstract: Large Language Models speed up software development but often produce flawed, non-production-ready code resulting in a controversy over their use. Existing AI coding tools lack transparency, confidence scores, and clear explanations. We present eXAI-CodeDebug, an explainable code analysis framework that combines static analysis with LLM-based detection to ensure production-quality code. It provides dual confidence scores, separately measuring certainty for detected issues and proposed fixes—plus detailed explanations and one-click automated corrections. The system covers seven issue categories, including hallucinations and security vulnerabilities, with customizable filters and thresholds. On 48 seeded issues, it achieves 100% precision and recall for Security and Hallucination, 85.7% F1 Score for Bug Detection, Quality, and Trustworthiness, and 69% Quick Fix success. Analysis averages 12.5 seconds per file and reduces debugging time from 6–23 minutes to under 2 seconds per issue.

title: A Cost-Efficient LDO Regulator Using a Dual-Loop Low-Impedance Buffer and a PT-Compensated Bias based on the Current-Subtraction Technique
authors: Tzung-Je Lee, Hung-Hsiang Chang, Han-Yi Chiu
abstract: This work proposes a cost-efficient, low-dropout (LDO) regulator. By employing the proposed novel Dual-Loop Low-Impedance (LI) Buffer, the second and third poles are shifted to higher frequencies. Thus, no additional resistor and capacitor (RC) frequency compensation is required to stabilize the proposed LDO regulator. Moreover, the resistors made of different materials are used in the Bandgap Reference circuit to realize a zero temperature coefficient (TC) while minimizing the area of BJTs and resistors, resulting in a cost-efficient LDO design. In addition, the proposed Current-Subtraction Technique is applied in the process-and-temperature (PT)-Compensated Bias circuit to generate a current with opposite PT characteristics, thereby improving the Phase Margin (PM) against the PT variations. The proposed design is implemented using a 0.18 μm BCD process. The Monte Carlo simulation results and measurement results from five fabricated chips are provided to validate the performance of the LDO regulator. The worst-case average settling time of the LDO regulator is measured as 0.744 μs at -25 oC. The measured peak current efficiency reaches 99.733%.

title: Investigating Cryptocurrency-Enabled Illicit Activities on the Arabic Deep/Dark Web
authors: Mohammad Shadi Alhakeem
abstract: Cyberspace presents a key factor for global connectivity, development, and innovation. Yet, the sophisticated use of anonymity-providing technologies, specifically the deep/dark web alongside cryptocurrency-enabled digital finance, has introduced a surge of complex challenges, often related to illegal and criminal endeavors. While existing literature has extensively explored these areas, focusing primarily on English-speaking environments and other specific languages, the relevant landscape of Arabic-language content remains significantly under-researched. Given that social and political dynamics of the Arab world differ substantially from other regions, this necessitates a different understanding of cyber-criminality. Our study addresses this research gap by exploring the relationship between the deep/dark web and cryptocurrencies focusing on Arabic content. We highlight how these anonymity-providing technologies enable and shape illicit activities specific to the Arabic-speaking world. A detailed investigation is conducted for related real-world cases to identify the types of illicit activities commonly associated with these technologies. Then we examine the specific methods by which they are leveraged within this ecosystem, emphasizing on the challenges faced by law enforcement agencies in combating these activities. Advanced search tools are used in this investigation to identify cryptocurrency-enabled illicit activities, focusing on the relevant Arabic content indexed recently. In addition, specialized tools are utilized to analyze the associated cryptocurrency transactions, delivering insights that provide enhanced understanding of the intersection between criminal activities and digital finance. Our investigation revealed many key findings which directly contribute to the body of knowledge necessary for authorities to track and combat the criminal misuse of cryptocurrencies within the Arab region.

title: Error-Driven Prompt Optimization for Arithmetic Reasoning A Code Generation Approach Using On-Premises Small Language Models on Tabular Data
authors: Árpád Pándy, Róbert Lakatos, András Hajdu
abstract: Recent advancements in artificial intelligence have sparked interest in industrial agents capable of supporting analysts in regulated sectors, such as finance and healthcare, within tabular data workflows. A key capability for such systems is performing accurate arithmetic operations on structured data while ensuring sensitive information never leaves secure, on-premises environments. Here, we introduce an error-driven optimization framework for arithmetic reasoning that enhances a Code Generation Agent (CGA), specifically applied to on-premises small language models (SLMs). Through a systematic evaluation of a leading SLMs (Qwen3 4B, Gemma 3N E4B, Gemma3 4B), we find that while the base model exhibits fundamental limitations in arithmetic tasks, our proposed error-driven method, which clusters erroneous predictions to refine prompt-rules iteratively, dramatically improves performance, elevating the model’s accuracy to 66.45%. Our results suggest that developing reliable, interpretable, and industrially deployable AI assistants can be achieved not only through costly fine-tuning but also via systematic, error-driven prompt optimization, enabling small models to surpass larger language models (GPT-3.5 Turbo) in a privacy-compliant manner.

title: Multiple Depot Split Delivery Vehicle Routing Problem with Separate Backhaul and Linehaul Customers
authors: Pitchaya Pongmanawut, Sirilak Phonin, Chulin Likasiri
abstract: This paper presents a heuristic approach for solving the Multi-Depot Split Delivery Vehicle Routing Problem with Separate Backhaul and Linehaul Customers (MDSDVRP-SBL). A modified mathematical model and a two-part heuristics framework are proposed, combining clustering and routing methods to enable split delivery. The clustering process assigns customers to depots while ensuring route feasibility and cost efficiency, and the routing method further optimizes the routes to facilitate split deliveries. Computational experiments on 33 benchmark instances show that the proposed approach lead to improvements in 5 cases and achieve 17 best-known solutions. Split delivery reduces transportation costs by up to 0.34% in benchmark problems and 5.32% in large-scale instances. The heuristics are also applied to a real-world agricultural residue management problem in northern Thailand, involving 974 backhaul customers, 127 depots, and 274 linehaul customers. Starting computations on the backhaul side reduces total transportation costs by 2.70%, while starting on the linehaul side achieves a 2.89% reduction with improved clustering efficiency. These findings highlight the scalability and effectiveness of the proposed heuristics for addressing complex routing problems. This study underscores the practical benefits of split delivery in reducing costs and optimizing vehicle utilization in multi-depot routing scenarios.

title: Power to Women: Leveraging Blockchain Technology for Sustainable Livelihood Enhancements in Fishing Communities
authors: Mojtaba Enayati, Sudha Arlikatti, Prasad Gudimetla, Maneesha Vinodini Ramesh
abstract: The contribution of women to the GDP of India is underestimated due to either their participation in informal livelihood sectors that are not captured through traditional data collection methods or their exclusion from primary livelihood sectors. The fisheries sector in Alappad Panchayat of Kerala, is no exception to this concern, as it is a very male-dominated sector. To address this issue, the UN has emphasized the need for development plans that prioritize women's inclusivity to accelerate the achievement of Sustainable Development Goal 5 of the 2030 Agenda. This study aims to understand, through a qualitative approach, the reasons behind the persistent poverty in the community and the potential for integrating women into the fisheries ecosystem. The study subsequently proposes a hybrid structure, combining a server and blockchain nodes, to create a fish trade platform that can be managed by women in marginalized communities. It leverages the Social Cognitive Theory (SCT) framework to understand influential factors necessary for a socio-cultural shift towards eradicating gender disparities. The platform is made available through mobile applications for fishermen and customers, offering a transparent value chain within a fair and competitive fish market. The study directly contributes to the United Nations' 2030 Agenda by aligning its findings with SDG 5 (women's empowerment) and SDG 8 (providing decent jobs).

title: Barriers to Meta AI Adoption Among Gen Z Women: A Study Based on Innovation Resistance Theory
authors: Akshay Ashok, Akhil Nair, Avinash Shivdas, Hari Krishnan R
abstract: Meta AI transforms digital communication by providing recommendations, content curation, and task support across all of Meta's apps. It influences growth in social platforms, virtual reality, and especially individualism-targeting techniques. This study explores barriers to Meta AI adoption among Gen Z women, using a conceptual model based on Innovation Resistance Theory. We analyzed data from 159 respondents to identify barriers impacting adoption. Based on Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM), the findings indicate that value barriers, risk barriers, perceived trust, and risk barriers hinder the adoption of Meta AI among Gen Z women.

title: Revolutionizing Women's Healthcare: Early UTI Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: M.S. Upamanyu, Sreevidya KV
abstract: Women all over the globe and particularly in developing countries like India face a significant health burden due to poor sanitation, with several infections posing extreme risks to their well-being. Among these, urinary tract infections (UTIs) are particularly prevalent, affecting approximately 38% of Indian women. The present research focuses on the prediction and early diagnosis of UTIs. While conventional diagnostic methods are time-consuming, machine learning (ML) offers an efficient and accurate alternative for identifying UTIs early. The study employs several ML models, including Decision Tree (DT), K-Nearest Neighbour (kNN), Logistic Regression, Linear Regression, and Support Vector Machine (SVM), to develop predictive tools based on patient data. Using data from 120 individuals, the models were trained and evaluated on key metrics such as accuracy, precision, F1 score, and Root Mean Squared Deviation (RMSD). The SVM model outperformed others, achieving a perfect accuracy and precision of 100%, with an F1 score of 1.0 and an RMSD of 0.0. These results highlight the potential of ML in revolutionizing UTI diagnostics, offering a quicker and more reliable approach compared to traditional methods. However, the lack of self-reporting, inadequate medical equipment and diagnostic tools in government hospitals and smaller clinics raises serious concerns. Hence, the findings of this study emphasize the urgent need for investment in healthcare resources and the adoption of advanced diagnostic technologies like AI and ML to detect and mitigate the impact of these infections.

title: Case Study: RISC-V Cores for Resource-Constrained Computing Systems Facing Neutrons
authors: P.-A. Peyronnet, L. H. Laurini, E. Atukpor, J. Guilbot, C. Costa, R. Possamai Bastos
abstract: This paper provides an evaluation of 3 resource-constrained RISC-V cores exposed to thermal neutron radiation. Lightweight edge computing systems were designed so as to study the impact of different architecture paradigms on SEU-related soft errors happening during Artificial Neural Network (ANN) inferences. The rate of soft errors of the most area-optimized architecture is 3.94 times lower than the most computationally performant one, suggesting that architectures minimizing area are more tolerant to neutron radiation.

title: Explainable AI for Autonomous Systems: A Framework for Trustworthy Decision-Making
authors: Isan Sahoo, Koushik Kumar Ganeeb, Pavan Nutalapati, Milan Parikh, Girish Ashok Gajwani, Rakesh Keshava, Bindu Harve
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: The growing reliance on autonomous systems across domains such as transportation, defense, logistics, and healthcare necessitate not only high-performance AI but also transparency in how autonomous decisions is made. Traditional AI models, especially deep neural networks, often operate as opaque black boxes, making it difficult for users, developers, and regulators to interpret or trust the underlying decisionmaking processes. To address this critical gap, this paper introduces a structured framework for embedding Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) into autonomous systems to promote interpretability, reliability, and human trust. Our framework enables explainability at multiple levels—perception, decision, and control—by integrating modelagnostic and model-specific techniques, such as SHAP, LIME, Grad-CAM, and attention-based visualization. It is designed to operate in real-time and adapt across various autonomous platforms, including simulated environments like CARLA for self-driving vehicles and AirSim for UAVs. The system is benchmarked using metrics that evaluate not just accuracy, but also fidelity, stability, and user interpretability of explanations. By focusing on both technical depth and usability, our approach supports critical functions such as post-incident analysis, debugging, human-in-the-loop verification, and regulatory transparency. This work contributes a replicable and scalable methodology for building autonomous agents that are not only intelligent but also explainable, transparent, and trustworthy in their operations.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for IoT Network Attack Classification Using Multi-Head CNN and GNN
authors: Maria Kiran
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC)
abstract: The rapid increase of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has introduced new challenges in securing network environments, particularly against complex and evolving cyber-attacks. In this paper, we propose a hybrid deep learning framework that leverages the strengths of both Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for accurate and scalable IoT network attack classification. Specifically, a Multi-Head One Dimensional (1D-CNN) is employed to extract hierarchical temporal features from flow-based traffic data, enhancing local pattern recognition across different kernel sizes. To capture inter-sample relationships and communication structure among IoT flows, a GNN is integrated using a k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) graph constructed over feature embeddings. The framework is evaluated using a multiclass subset of the CICIDS-2017 dataset comprising benign and various DoS attack types. Results demonstrate that the hybrid CNN-GNN model outperforms conventional baselines, achieving high accuracy and improved generalization with limited data. To ensure scalability, the GNN operates on a data subset using sparse graph construction, significantly reducing memory and computational costs. The framework also includes comparative visualizations such as confusion matrices, performance metrics, and radar plots. This work highlights the effectiveness of graphenhanced representations in deep learning architectures for robust IoT threat detection.

title: Fault Prediction for Power Information Systems Based on Hierarchical Vertical Federated Contrastive Learning
authors: Chaoyang Qu, Chong Wang
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 15th International Conference on Communication and Network Security
abstract: None

title: Symptom-Aware Adaptation of CLIP for Few-Shot Medical Image Classification
authors: Zexiang Li, Wenxu Wang, Chengpu Liu, Jiawen He, Youwei Zhao, Yili Zhai, Ruichun Tang
abstract: Vision-language pre-training model yields powerful general-purpose representations, yet its direct application to medical images is hindered by two key gaps: (i) low sensitivity to fine-grained pathological cues and (ii) semantic misalignment with structured clinical terminology. We propose SITSA, a framework that adapts a frozen CLIP backbone for data-scarce medical tasks without full fine-tuning. SITSA introduces three synergistic components: (1) MedVisExtractor, a depthwise-separable convolutional adapter that recalibrates patch tokens to emphasize subtle lesions; (2) SymSemLoRA, which injects low-rank updates into the text encoder conditioned on large-language-model-generated prompts that encode texture, location, and shape attributes; and (3) Prototype-Guided Alignment, a cross-modal module that binds image patches and prompt tokens via shared learnable prototypes. Extensive experiments on COVID-QU-Ex, OCTMNIST, and DERMAMNIST demonstrate that SITSA surpasses thirteen state-of-the-art baselines in both few-shot and base-to-novel generalization, achieving the highest harmonic mean accuracy while keeping the backbone frozen. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/enfantsRichesDeprimes/sitsa</uri>.

title: A Study on Improving Superconducting Machine Performance by Focusing on End Windings
authors: Chao Luo, Jiabo Shou, Jien Ma, Youtong Fang
abstract: The current topology for superconducting machines aligns the effective stator length with the straight section of the rotor racetrack magnet, resulting in wasted superconducting material at the magnet's ends. Moreover, the ends of the superconducting magnet produce a substantial magnetic field, resulting in significant electromagnetic forces on the stator winding ends under rated operating conditions. This paper comprehensively analyzes and addresses the above issues in superconducting machines by proposing two improved stator end structures. Firstly, the difference between non-superconducting and superconducting machines on the stator side electromagnetic force is summarized by theoretical analysis. Then, based on the H-formulation, a three-dimensional numerical model reveals that the minimum critical current in traditional superconducting machines occurs at the end of the racetrack winding's straight section. The improved design in this paper increases it effectively. Moreover, it is demonstrated that without any modification of the high price of superconducting materials employed for the rotor, the output power of the proposed two structures can be effectively increased by 11.3% and 25.9%, respectively, while minimizing electromagnetic force at the stator end winding. A comprehensive comparison of the two proposed design modifications indicates that extending the stator's effective length and incorporating partial magnetic teeth at its ends is the more advantageous strategy. Finally, the experiment indicates that the proposed structure in this study enhances the critical current and mitigates the issue of stator end electromagnetic force.

title: Boosting Instance Awareness Via Cross-View Correlation With 4D Radar and Camera for 3D Object Detection
authors: Xiaokai Bai, Lianqing Zheng, Si-Yuan Cao, Xiaohan Zhang, Zhe Wu, Beinan Yu, Fang Wang, Jie Bai, Hui-Liang Shen
abstract: 4D millimeter-wave radar has emerged as a promising sensing modality for autonomous driving due to its robustness and affordability. However, its sparse and weak geometric cues make reliable instance activation difficult, limiting the effectiveness of existing radar–camera fusion paradigms. BEV-level fusion offers global scene understanding but suffers from weak instance focus, while perspective-level fusion captures instance details but lacks holistic context. To address these limitations, we propose SIFormer, a scene–instance aware transformer for 3D object detection using 4D radar and camera. SIFormer first suppresses background noise during view transformation through segmentation- and depth-guided localization. It then introduces a cross-view activation mechanism that injects 2D instance cues into BEV space, enabling reliable instance awareness under weak radar geometry. Finally, a transformer-based fusion module aggregates complementary image semantics and radar geometry for robust perception. As a result, with the aim of enhancing instance awareness, SIFormer bridges the gap between the two paradigms, combining their complementary strengths to address inherent sparse nature of radar and improve detection accuracy. Experiments demonstrate that SIFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance on View-of-Delft, TJ4DRadSet and NuScenes datasets. The code will be made publicly available.

title: A Radially Outward Extended Permanent Magnet Overhang Technique for Electromagnetic Performance Enhancement in Permanent Magnet Machines
authors: Soheil Yousefnejad, Ebrahim Amiri, Parviz Rastgoufard
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Industry Applications
abstract: This paper proposes a structural approach to leverage the unused space created by the stator's end winding, with the goal of enhancing the machine's torque. To accomplish this, Permanent Magnets (PMs) are extended beyond the active stack, forming an overhang structure that boosts stator flux linkage and enhances torque delivery. This enables the design of a smaller motor that delivers the same torque output, offering a higher torque/power density. The proposed structure is also optimized and compared with alternative designs under the constraint of equal magnet volume to highlight its effectiveness. Furthermore, a Magnetic Equivalent Circuit (MEC) model of the proposed structure is developed and validated using Finite Element (FE) analysis. Experimental validation is also conducted, and the results are compared with the conventional overhang configuration.

title: Optimization-Based Robust Control for Momentum Wheel Systems Subject to Large-Scale Power Supply Fluctuations
authors: Peng Li, Ruihao Sun, Fan Zhang, Nursultan Meirambekuly, Jianguo Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Voltage fluctuations in unregulated microsatellite buses introduce strong coupled nonlinearities in momentum wheel systems, which consist of power supply (PS) and permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) subsystems. Voltage fluctuation-induced coupling can dramatically affect the performance of momentum wheel systems. To address this specific challenge, this paper proposed a systematically dual-loop controller based on feedback linearization (DLC-FL), mixing <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{2}/H_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> and linear quadratic regulator (LQR) control strategies. For the inner-loop control, the affine nonlinear PS-PMSM model is linearized using differential geometric techniques, and mixed <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{2}/H_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> control is applied to this linearized coupled model to guarantee robust stability and dynamic response characteristics. Regarding the outer-loop control, we propose a LQR framework integrated with Gaussian Distribution Particle Swarm Optimization (GDPSO) algorithm for parameter tuning. Simulation results show that the proposed controller has a 64% reduction in speed step response time, 51% mitigation of DC bus voltage fluctuations, and the proposed GDPSO achieves 50% faster convergence compared to conventional particle swarm optimization methods. Hardware experiments also validate superior performance, with a total harmonic distortion of only 7.96%, offering an effective solution for high-precision control of multivariable nonlinear electric servo systems.

title: Holo-Code: A Robust and Statistically Lossless Holographic Watermarking Framework for AIGC in Cloud-Edge IoT
authors: Hang Fu, Yu Cui, Licheng Wang, Junke Duan, Liehuang Zhu
abstract: The proliferation of AI-Generated Content (AIGC) within cloud-edge IoT ecosystems necessitates robust provenance mechanisms to ensure copyright protection and forensic traceability. However, the open distribution mechanism of AIGC exposes proprietary assets to risks of unauthorized replication and misuse. While watermarking offers a solution, existing schemes face a critical dilemma in IoT environments: they are either computationally expensive for edge deployment or structurally fragile against the dual threats of unpredictable IoT transmission failures and post-distribution image manipulations. To overcome these limitations, we propose Holo-Code, a robust and statistically lossless watermarking architecture based on a decoupled channel-coding paradigm. We introduce a holographic topological transformation to globally disperse watermark information across the latent representation. This mechanism mathematically converts structural data loss into uniform random erasures, which are robustly recovered via concatenated error correction coding. Furthermore, Holo-Code incorporates a distribution whitening mechanism that ensures the embedded signal is statistically indistinguishable from the standard Gaussian prior, thereby preserving the high fidelity of generated images. Extensive experiments validate that Holo-Code achieves a superior trade-off between robustness, quality, and efficiency. The framework guarantees reliable traceability across multiple generative models even under extreme composite degradations, seamlessly surviving heavy quantization from edge fallback mechanisms combined with severe structural occlusion. Moreover, as a training-free intervention, Holo-Code executes the complete forensic extraction on standard CPUs and achieves highly efficient identity retrieval against a database of one million users, establishing it as a practical solution for real-time AIGC provenance in large-scale IoT networks.

title: From Uncertainty to Decision: An Intelligent Predictive Maintenance Approach Leveraging Fuzzy Bayesian Networks for Critical IT Components
authors: Mohammed Said Boukhryss, Abdellah Azmani, Khalid Jebari Hassani, Yousra Bouassab, Nihad Lechhab Ouadrassi
abstract: Information Technology (IT) systems are essential for ensuring the continuous availability of digital services, yet they remain vulnerable to unexpected failures that can disrupt operations and generate significant costs. To address these risks, predictive maintenance (PdM) strategies are required to anticipate failures and preserve reliable performance. This study develops a PdM approach for critical IT systems. A hybrid methodology is introduced, combining Bayesian networks (BNs) to model intricate causal dependencies among various system parameters and fuzzy logic (FL) to calculate the conditional probabilities, which are then integrated into the BNs to accurately predict potential system failures. Model validation was performed through the verification of three axioms. Six degradation scenarios are then generated, followed by two sensitivity analyses: a partial analysis to identify key parameters influencing individual components, and a global analysis to determine parameters affecting overall system degradation. Finally, an event tree analysis is applied to evaluate the effectiveness of different maintenance strategies, providing insights to minimize failure risks and improve system reliability. The results indicate that the system enters a critical state once a major component fails, and the risk of total system failure becomes almost certain when multiple components degrade simultaneously.

title: Energy- and Area-Efficient MTJ-Based Nonvolatile Register with a Differential Information Storing Scheme
authors: Tomoo Yoshida, Masanori Natsui, Takahiro Hanyu
abstract: This paper presents a circuit configuration for a low‑write‑energy and area‑efficient nonvolatile register using magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ) devices, targeting persistent computation in intermittent computing environments. The proposed data retention scheme, named the differential information storing scheme (DISS), stores one bit of information using the resistance difference between a pair of adjacent MTJ devices, thereby minimizing the number of MTJ devices required. This design choice allows the proposed register to reduce both circuit area and backup energy consumption, while enabling low-latency backup and restore operations that are independent of the register bit width. Functional simulations using a 55‑nm CMOS/MTJ‑hybrid process technology demonstrate that, for register sizes ranging from 2 to 32 bits, DISS achieves superior energy efficiency, area efficiency, and operating speed compared with conventional designs. Furthermore, post‑layout simulations quantitatively show that the proposed configuration reduces backup energy by up to 45.9% and circuit area by up to 23.4% relative to a baseline nonvolatile register.

title: Statistical Diagnostics and Forecast Accuracy: Comparing Time-Series, Machine-Learning, and Hybrid Models for Drug Sales
authors: Hüseyin Abaci
abstract: Pharmacy-level drug sales forecasting is critical for reducing inventory costs, minimizing waste, and maintaining continuity of healthcare services; however, it remains a challenging problem because sales time series are shaped by factors such as seasonal illnesses, promotions/discounts, epidemic waves, and supply disruptions. In this study, SARIMA, ETS/Holt–Winters, machine learning (Elastic Net, SVR, XGBoost), and hybrid (residual-learning) approaches were compared across four therapeutic categories with distinct real-world sales patterns: Antibiotics, Painkillers (analgesics), Antidepressants, and Cancer medications. Guided by statistical diagnostic pre-analysis (ADF, ACF/PACF, trend/seasonality etc.), we first formed an expectation of which model would be suitable for each category and then quantified how strongly the sample results confirmed that expectation. The proposed framework aims to build a leakage-safe and practically deployable forecasting pipeline under small-data conditions by stacking a second layer that learns the non-linear residual component on top of a SARIMA backbone that captures seasonal structure. In addition, a stage-wise feature/ablation analysis isolates the contribution of time-series features such as lags, differences, and seasonal encodings; performance is reported using relative MAE/RMSE and R², and model differences are assessed via Diebold–Mariano tests. The findings show that standalone SARIMA provides a strong baseline, while hybrid models markedly reduce error. RelMAE dropped from 5.84% to 4.21% for Antibiotics (28% reduction), from 4.41% to 3.48% for Antidepressants (21%), from 12.45% to 8.03% for Cancer (36%), and from 7.25% to 4.96% for Painkillers (32%). The best hybrid (SARIMA+XGBoost) raised R² from 0.73 to 0.85 for Antibiotics, from 0.04 to 0.47 for Cancer, and from 0.21 to 0.60 for Painkillers; for Antidepressants, SARIMA+SVR achieved R² = 0.87 vs. 0.83.

title: Ensemble Image and Text for Unsupervised Domain Adaptation Using Vision Language Models
authors: Qi Jia, Fei Du, Zulong Lin, Jing Guo, Yun Yang
abstract: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (UDA) has emerged as a pivotal technique for enhancing machine learning models’ performance in unlabeled target domains with domain shifts. This technique is fundamentally achieved by aligning the domain distributions of source and target domains within a latent feature space, thereby enhancing model robustness across heterogeneous data distributions. However, the inherent discrepancy between source and target domain distributions poses significant challenges in identifying the optimal latent space. Furthermore, projecting both domains into suboptimal latent spaces may induce substantial semantic information loss, particularly compromising discriminative feature representations critical for final tasks. In this paper, Our systematic analysis reveals that natural language representations inherently possess stronger semantic abstraction capabilities than visual features in natural images. As a result, natural language tends to have a smaller domain shifts. Motivated by this discovery, we propose a novel model that systematically transforms visual patterns into structured linguistic representations. This cross-modal translation mechanism leverages the invariant semantic properties of natural language to mitigate domain shifts while preserving task-critical semantic hierarchies. Our model leverages the inherent abstraction capacity of linguistic structures to enhance cross-domain generalization, effectively bridging the visual-semantic gap in unsupervised adaptation scenarios. Our model comprises three core components: (1) a text classification branch translating images to text for prediction, (2) an image adaptation branch supplementing visual details, and (3) an ensemble mechanism reconciling text abstraction with visual granularity through mismatch detection. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets validate the effectiveness of our model, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

title: A Novel Doppler Resilient Waveform Design for MIMO Radar Based on Complete Complementary Codes and Zero Correlation Zone Sequence Sets
authors: Tzu-Chieh Kao, Cheng-Yu Pai, Chao-Yu Chen
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel design of Doppler resilient (DR) waveforms for multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) radar systems to enhance target detection performance. The proposed waveforms are constructed from a new class of sequence sets that combine the properties of the complete complementary code (CCC) and the zero correlation zone (ZCZ) sequence set, termed the CCC-ZCZ sequence set. The ambiguity function of the proposed CCC-ZCZ-based waveforms demonstrates superior Doppler tolerance and lower range sidelobes. Additionally, a systematic construction based on generalized Boolean functions (GBFs) is proposed to generate CCC-ZCZ sequence sets with various parameters. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed waveforms based on the CCC-ZCZ sequence set achieve superior detection performance compared to existing alternatives in MIMO radar systems.

title: Linear-Exponential Decay Frequency Curve for Unit-Specific Response in Frequency-Constrained Unit Commitment
authors: Guocan Yan, Lei Yan, Yan Meng, Jiayu Han, Zuyi Li
abstract: High renewable penetration and declining inertia jeopardize power system frequency security, especially in terms of primary frequency response (PFR). This paper proposes a linear-exponential decay frequency curve (LED-FC) to approximate pre-nadir system frequency dynamics and efficiently assess unit-specific nadir response (FNR) while preserving unit heterogeneity. Accounting for PFR reserve headroom and internal saturation, a frequency-constrained unit commitment (FCUC) model with bilinear terms is formulated, where the frequency nadir is an endogenous decision variable enforced by the nadir power sufficiency condition. To handle disturbance uncertainty, a hybrid scenario–chance-constrained (HSCC) framework captures both discrete RES tripping events and the continuous uncertainty in disturbance magnitude. The bilinear constraints are handled via the normalized multiparametric disaggregation technique (NMDT), yielding a relaxed mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) formulation, which is further tightened by a frequency nadir search (FNS) algorithm to reduce relaxation errors and certify optimality within a prescribed tolerance. Case studies on modified IEEE 39-bus and 300-bus systems validate that the proposed approach achieves accurate nadir assessment, physically feasible reserve allocation, and computational scalability.

title: Fast Algorithm for Zernike Moments Invariants in Scene Matching
authors: Yanjun Fu, Xinhai Song
abstract: Zernike moments are well-suited for unmanned aerial vehicle scene matching because of their rotation invariance and robustness to noise. However, their high computational complexity poses challenges for meeting the real-time requirements of navigation systems. To address this issue and enhance the real-time performance of scene matching navigation, this study proposed a hybrid matching strategy. This strategy, based on Wee's high-precision "GMs-to-ZMs" Zernike moment calculation method, combines offline and online computational phases to reduce online matching time during flight. In the offline phase, we precompute all the required multi-order cumulative geometric moment tables and a binomial coefficients table. In the online phase, these precomputed lookup tables reduce the computational complexity of the Zernike moments for each reference sub-image from O(n2) to O(1). Furthermore, the introduction of normalized moments enhances the adaptability of the algorithm to scale variations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves real-time performance while maintaining matching accuracy, offering an effective solution for the practical application of Zernike moments in unmanned aerial vehicle navigation.

title: Hidden-Uncertainty Assessment via Non-Verbalized Signatures for LLM Medical QA_supp1-3682338.mp4
authors: ENRICO D'URSO

title: An Analysis of Time-Domain Period Jitter due to Random Telegraph Noise in Phase-Locked Loops
authors: Caroline P. Garcia, Gilson I. Wirth
abstract: Charge trapping and de-trapping phenomena are major sources of noise and aging in nanometer-scale devices, degrading their performance and reliability. Random Telegraph Noise (RTN) is a stochastic mechanism arising from the continuous capture and emission events of charge carriers by traps, causing the drain current (or threshold voltage) amplitude to fluctuate randomly from one instant in time to another. These fluctuations may cause variations in the oscillation period length (jitter) of oscillators. This work evaluates the time-domain period jitter induced by RTN in a charge-pump phase-locked loop (PLL), examining the relationship between the traps' corner (Lorentzian) or switching frequency, the PLL loop bandwidth, and the resulting jitter amplitude. It was observed that traps with switching frequencies close to the loop bandwidth of the closed-loop system lead to higher amplitudes of period jitter.

title: A Systematic Literature Review on Digital Activism: Empowering Women Through Technology in Developing Countries
authors: Fatou Mbye, Ousman Badjie, Christopher Coley, William Paul Simmons
abstract: This systematic literature review explores the impact of digital activism and Information and Communication Technology (ICT) in the empowerment of women in developing countries. Using the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) framework, relevant academic articles published between January 2014 and July 2024 were identified through a comprehensive search of Google Scholar and Scopus. The search strategy employed Boolean operators with keywords such as “Women Empowerment,” “Digital Activism,” “Technology,” and “Policy.” A total of 30 studies met the inclusion criteria and were analyzed to uncover the impact of digital tools on women's income generation, entrepreneurship, and social participation. The findings reveal that access to ICT significantly enhances women's economic opportunities and contributes to gender equality. However, challenges such as socio-cultural barriers, the digital divide, and inadequate policy frameworks hinder the full potential of digital activism in these regions. The review highlights key trends, including the growing use of social media for activism and the engagement of male allies in gender equality campaigns. Policy recommendations include enhancing digital literacy, improving ICT infrastructure, and developing gender-sensitive policies to support women's digital empowerment. This study underscores the critical role of digital activism in fostering sustainable development and advocates for targeted interventions to bridge existing gaps.

title: Trade-Space Exploration Analysis for a System Incorporating Parametric Cost Mathematical Models: A Case Study of Satellite Anomalies
authors: Muhammad Aryss Zafran Shahrul Ariffin, Nadirah Abdul Rahim, Abdul Mutholib, Mahayaudin M. Mansor
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: Satellites are essential for modern applications such as communication, navigation, and Earth observation. However, their operation in the harsh conditions of space makes them susceptible to anomalies and failures, leading to significant operational and financial losses. This research aims to address these challenges by identifying recurring satellite problems, optimizing the trade-offs between cost, design life, insurance loss, and performance using trade-space exploration analysis to improve satellite reliability and cost-effectiveness. The satellite anomalies that are considered in this research are antenna, payload, attitude control system, and power system. These four anomalies are selected based on the most failed subsystems from the Seradata database. Parametric cost models: Exponential and Weibull are analyzed using the trade-space exploration (TSE) technique, focusing on selecting the best design option to minimize failures and associated costs, including launch expenses and insurance losses. The performance of each model is compared using Root Mean Square Error (RMSE). From the results, it can be deduced that the Exponential model has the lowest RMSE value for each component. The RMSE values of the Exponential model are much lower with cost versus design life values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$42.21,59.88,31.27$</tex>, and 32.00 compared to the Weibull model's values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$226.54,255.30,316.75$</tex>, and 217.83, respectively. The findings highlight the key factors contributing to satellite anomalies and demonstrate how the developed models can guide decision-making in satellite design and operations. By balancing cost, performance, and design life, this research provides valuable insights for designing cost-effective satellites while enhancing their operational success.

title: Empowering Women Entrepreneurs Through Blockchain-Enabled Credit Guarantee Schemes: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Animesh Kumar, Nidhi Natrajan
abstract: The paper aims to explore the potential of Blockchain in disbursement of the Credit Guarantee Scheme loans to the Women Entepreneurs in order to promote gender equality leading to the achievement of the UN Sustainable Development Goals 2030. Moreover, it also attempts to study the relationship between Blockchain, Artificial Intelligence and financial inclusion with special reference to the Women Entrepreneurship in the MSMEs (Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises) in the context of India. It examines the potential of blockchain technology in providing Credit Guarantee Schemes tailored specifically for the Women Entrepreneurs in India. By addressing traditional barriers to the credit, this paper proposes the role of DAOs i.e. Decentralized Autonomous Organizations that enhances ease and transparency in the credit disbursement, reducing risk for lenders, and opens up for new and multiple avenues for female - led businesses. Furthermore, this study synthesizes current literature to offer insights into blockchain's role in fostering inclusive financial environment using the Bibliometric Analysis. This qualitative research work fits with a key gap in the existing research by the key challenges in the Blockchain adoption for the disbursement of the Credit Guarantee Scheme for the women entrepreneurs in the MSMEs by developing a valuable framework to model this important topic. To the best of my knowledge this is the first paper to address these financial concerns of women entrepreneurs in the MSME arena with the usage of Blockchain technology.

title: MicroMaps: Ontology-Driven Semantic Geospatial Data Fusion Approach for IoT Integration and Decision Making in Smart
authors: Abdelrhman Rayis, Marco Ortolani, Ruusa David, Raphael Hirschi, Göksel Mısırlı
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC)
abstract: Smart cities generate large amounts of Internet of Things data from diverse sources, often presented in varying formats and with inconsistent quality, complicating effective data management and interoperability. Despite recent advancements related to geospatial data standards and interoperability frameworks, there is a need for modular and comprehensive semantic frameworks that can leverage distributed architectures to manage specific geospatial functions, such as real-time disaster response mapping and resource allocation in smart cities. This paper presents the MicroMaps framework designed to enhance decision-making in smart city projects by addressing geospatial interoperability issues in cloud-native geographical information systems through semantic integration. MicroMaps employs semantic models and microservices to standardise data into an ontology-driven knowledge graph for real-time processing. This geographical information system provides an end-to-end solution to integrate, distribute and visualise geospatial data. A case study at Keele University demonstrates this framework's capability to unify and analyse diverse datasets, including real-time sensor data and historical time-series data on energy consumption and climate conditions. The findings underscore the framework's ability to uncover spatial patterns, thereby improving spatial data infrastructure and decision-making.

title: Handover Algorithm for UAV-BS 5G Network
authors: Ahmad Zaki Aiman Abdul Rashid, Azita Laily Yusof, Norsuzila Ya'acob
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: Deploying Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAVs) as a base station (UAV-BS) for extending network coverage is seen as a prominent solution for supporting the 5G and future communications demands such as faster speeds, lower latency, improved connectivity and coverage. One of the main problem of communication utilizing UAV-BS is the signal coverage provided to users on the ground depends on the UAV-BS characteristics. In order to maintain good signal coverage for users, an efficient handover algorithm that takes into consideration UAVs characteristics such as flying altitude, and, the distances between UAV-BS is required. This study proposes a handover algorithm for the UAV-BS network by taking into account the handover threshold (RSRPthreshold), UAV-BS flying altitude and distance between UAV-BS. Three outputs were collected and evaluated from the proposed algorithm such as average RSRP for UAV-BS, the handover ping-pong rate, and the total number of handover events. Results from the simulation shows that the proposed handover algorithm capable of reducing handover ping-pong rate when compared to other researcher's method.

title: Towards Smart Farming an IoT Based Optimized Agriculture System
authors: Leena Affouri, Mariam Hamad, Salwa Fayad, Wasel Ghanem, Ali Kadhim Lwas, Ashraf Al-Rimawi, Mohamed Hadi Habaebi, Alhareth Mohammed Zyoud
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: Agriculture in Palestine is considered the most significant sector that contributes to the gross domestic product. It is the pillar of the Palestinian economy and central to Palestinian identity. Reviewing recent reports by international organizations and the Ministry of Agriculture reveals the significant destruction in this sector. Recent reports by the United Nations show that less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> of households in Palestine were food secure for the last three years. Smart agriculture is introduced as a solution to improve the performance of the crop production process and secure the food sector. This study aims to integrate the Internet of Things (IoT) and new technologies to enhance the agricultural production sector in Palestine. Three popular plants were selected, tomato, lettuce and cucumber. The outcomes of this study will save the farmer time and minimize the effort required in the production process.

title: Evaluating an Analog Test Pattern Generation Tool by Means of Hardware-Emulated Fault Injection
authors: Luis A. Duarte, Lucas B. Zilch, Állan F. G. Ferreira, Marcelo S. Lubaszewski, Tiago R. Balen
abstract: This work presents a hardware-emulated fault injection experiment designed to evaluate a previously proposed test pattern generation tool for analog circuits. The tool processes the SPICE netlist of the circuit under test, performing iterative fault simulations and analyzing AC, DC, and transient responses at internal nodes and primary outputs. Its output is a test sequence that identifies optimal stimuli and observation points to maximize fault coverage. For experimental validation, a simple operational amplifier was implemented using MOS transistors from a commercial device. The SPICE netlist and corresponding transistor models were provided as input to the tool. The generated test sequence was then applied to the hardware circuit while open and short faults were injected directly into the hardware, considering the same fault model which was previously simulated. Experimental results show good fault coverage and good agreement with the simulations. Discrepancies were observed in the behavior of certain hard-to-detect faults — particularly open faults at transistor gate terminals — between the simulation and the hardware implementation.

title: Case Study: A Robust Bootloader System with Support for Over-the-Air Firmware Updates in CubeSat Payloads
authors: João Sobreira, Wendell Silva, Cícero Oliveira, Marcus Bezerra, Jarbas Silveira
abstract: Over-the-air (OTA) firmware updates are challenging for CubeSat payloads due to intermittent communication links, constrained system resources, and the critical risk of system failure in the event of an unsuccessful update. This paper presents a practical case study of a bootloader-based mechanism that enables OTA firmware updates in a CubeSat payload. The proposed architecture uses a dual-bank flash organization and metadata-based control to separate firmware reception from activation, ensuring that only validated images are executed. Firmware integrity is enforced through packet-level integrity checks and a robust cryptographic verification of the complete firmware image, while rollback mechanisms preserve a knowngood firmware state under interrupted transfers, corrupted data, or power-loss events. Experimental results under representative fault scenarios indicate that the approach enables safe and consistent in-orbit firmware updates.

title: Hypothesis Testing on the 3D-DFT of Backward Compatible FMCW-OFDM for V2X ISAC
authors: Shahab Ehsanfar
abstract: This article presents a unified detection framework for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) using fused frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) and orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) waveforms. A 3D-DFT-based monostatic multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) radar model is developed, and closed-form expressions for detection and false-alarm probabilities are derived under Neyman–Pearson and generalized likelihood ratio tests. A sub-optimal best linear unbiased estimator (BLUE) fusion combines FMCW and OFDM radar returns, providing up to 3 dB additive signal-to-noise-ratio (SNR) gain while remaining backward compatible with existing OFDM standards. The impact of waveform peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) and power-amplifier back-off is analyzed, and the computational complexity and energy proxy of the 3D-DFT plus BLUE receiver are evaluated. Simulations confirm the theoretical analysis, demonstrating improved sensing accuracy under realistic mmWave MIMO configurations.

title: Phase Laser Ranging Radar Based on IoT Communication
authors: Ketty Siti Salamah, Trie Maya Kadarina, Puput Dani Prasetyo Adi, Rachmat Muwardi, Rizky Rahmatullah, Mirna Yunita
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: This paper introduces and implements an Internet of Things (IoT) system that seamlessly integrates UDP protocol communication with multi-scale phase laser radar technology. At its core, the system employs an FPGA to tackle the challenges of high-speed laser signal acquisition and realtime processing. To overcome the bottleneck associated with rapid laser ranging, an innovative multi-scale phase ranging algorithm based on dual-frequency modulation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(150 \text{MHz}$</tex> and 0.5 MHz) has been devised, achieving a ranging precision of 0.01 meters over a distance of 0 to 300 meters, with efficient processing realized through FPGA-based hardware filters and phase-locked loops (PLL). The system leverages the low-latency nature of the UDP protocol to establish a robust transmission architecture comprising a high-speed Ethernet MAC, UDP communication core, data buffer FIFO, and interface logic, thereby ensuring stable communication between the FPGA and the host computer. To further expand its application scenarios, a host computer platform has been developed in Python. Experimental results demonstrate that the system excels in target detection, real-time monitoring, and high-precision ranging, while consuming a modest amount of hardware resources-only 20 DSP units, 2132 LUTs, 2408 registers, and 7 block memories. This work offers a novel solution for the integration of precise LiDAR imaging with the Internet of Things, and future research will focus on incorporating intelligent algorithms to further enhance system robustness in complex scenarios.

title: SDN Based Firewall Using RYU Controller
authors: Salem Omar Sati, Zainab Senan Attarbashi
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer and Communication Engineering (ICCCE)
abstract: Software-Defined Networking (SDN) has emerged as an essential solution for centralized and flexible network management. However, security remains a critical concern, particularly in dynamic firewall policy enforcement. This paper proposes an SDN-based firewall using the RYU Controller to enable real-time traffic filtering, enhancing network security while minimizing latency and jitter. A custom C#-based management tool dynamically configures firewall rules, which are rigorously evaluated across different Mininet topologies of 10, 20, and 40 hosts to assess scalability. Performance metrics focus on Round-Trip Time (RTT), analyzing both Layer 3 (IP-based) and Layer 4 (HTTP/ICMP) filtering efficiency. Experimental results show the system's effectiveness in enforcing preferable access control with negligible latency overhead, even under increased network loads. The study highlights SDN's capability to deliver scalable, adaptive security solutions and provides insights for future research in large-scale and cloud environments.

title: Digital Conservation and Gender Dynamics: Exploring Technological Interventions in the Preservation of Paraya Art-Form 'Karinkali'
authors: Malavika Nair, Jayakrishnan Narayanan
abstract: The paper examines how gender, education, and cultural shifts in the Paraya community and the potential technology interventions shape the preservation avenues of Karinkali art form of the Paraya Community in Kerala. The research explores potential digital interventions that can contribute towards documenting the disappearing art form of Karinkali and argues how Augmented Reality (AR), Virtual Reality (VR) and other digital documentation techniques play a significant role in this process. The qualitative study employing in-depth interviews with the practitioners and performers of the art form analyzes the historical and mythical contexts of the art form, the influence of gender norms on artistic expression, and the function of technology in preserving the cultural heritage. The findings of the study suggest that while technology opens new avenues for education and conservation, there are still significant barriers preventing women from performing Karinkali art from.

title: AI-Based TEL Tools in Classroom: Exploring Challenges of Secondary School Teachers
authors: Gayathri Pothancheri, Punya Sankar, Bicky Kuriappan, Ishika Ishika
abstract: The integration of ICT and digital technologies is expanding quickly. When it comes to the adoption of AI-based Technology-Enhanced Learning (TEL) technologies in Indian secondary school environments, the pace seems to be crawling. Previous research studies show that, since these technologies are developed predominantly by men, there is a risk that they may not adequately address the needs and concerns of female teachers. Hence, the unique challenges female teachers face to the adoption need to be explored and identified; then only they can be countered with practical solutions. This study tries to explore the challenges the secondary school teachers' face in adopting the AI-based TEL tools in their daily teaching practice. It employs a mixed-methods approach with surveys and semi-structured interviews of 99 teachers by tailoring the questionnaire from a similar study. The analysis of the responses revealed that the teachers see AI-based TEL tools as beneficial for personalized learning, but adoption is hindered by infrastructure, training, and complexity concerns. Teachers' perceptions also varied, highlighting the need for tailored support. However, limitations like geographical focus and the self-reported nature of data call for further research in diverse contexts and with a longitudinal approach. The study emphasises the need for targeted training for the secondary school teachers and updating the curriculum, to facilitate effective AI adoption.
